State government, excluding education and hospitals

title: Personal financial advisors
exposure_rationale: Personal financial advisors do substantial knowledge work — analyzing portfolios, researching investments, and creating financial plans — that AI can increasingly assist with or automate, and robo-advisors already handle simpler cases. However, the core value proposition heavily depends on interpersonal trust, relationship-building, and sales skills that are difficult for AI to replicate, and the BLS itself notes robo-advisors will only partially temper demand. The occupation sits at a moderate-to-high exposure level where AI augments productivity significantly but the human relationship element remains a meaningful barrier to full displacement.

ai_paths.reason: Transfers expertise in budgeting, resource allocation, and client management.
Leverages deep experience in sensitive, high-stakes interpersonal counseling.
Applies business operations, compliance, and long-term planning skills.
Utilizes case management and advocacy for individual client welfare.
Transfers goal-setting and personalized plan development for clients.
Applies performance analysis and strategic planning to individual development.
Uses experience navigating families through complex, emotional life transitions.
Leverages ability to explain complex information and track progress.

education_detail: Personal financial advisors typically need a bachelor’s degree, although employers usually do not require a specific course of study. However, commonfields of degreeincludebusiness,social science, ormathematics. Courses in investments, taxes, estate planning, and risk management may be helpful.
certifications: Personal financial advisors who directly buy or sell stocks, bonds, or insurance policies, or who provide specific investment advice, may need a combination of licenses that varies with the products they sell. In addition to being required to have those licenses, advisors in small firms that manage clients’ investments must be registered with state regulators, and those in large firms must be registered with theU.S. Securities and Exchange Commission(SEC). Information on state licensing board requirements is available fromCareerOneStop. Certifications may enhance a personal financial advisor’s reputation and help bring in new clients. Information on certifications is available fromCareerOneStop.

industries.name: Securities, commodity contracts, and other financial investments and related activities

title: Pest control workers
exposure_rationale: Pest control work is fundamentally physical and location-dependent, requiring workers to travel to client sites, inspect premises, apply chemicals, set traps, and navigate confined spaces. AI might assist with scheduling, route optimization, record-keeping, or identifying pest species from photos, but these are peripheral to the core job. The hands-on nature of pesticide application, physical inspection, and structural barrier installation cannot be meaningfully automated by AI.
education_detail: Pest control workers typically need high school diploma or equivalent to enter the occupation. Employers may consider experienced candidates for some pest control jobs.
certifications: Most states require pest control workers to be licensed. Licensure requirements vary by state, but workers usually must complete training and pass an exam. Some states have additional requirements, such as having a high school diploma or equivalent. States may have additional requirements for applicators. Check with your state regulatory agency for more information.

title: Petroleum engineers
exposure_rationale: Petroleum engineering involves substantial knowledge work — data analysis, reservoir modeling, well design, and optimization — that AI can increasingly assist with or automate, making individual engineers more productive. However, a significant portion of the job requires physical site visits, hands-on troubleshooting at drilling locations, and coordination with field workers in unpredictable environments, which limits full AI displacement. The highly specialized, safety-critical nature of the work and the need for professional judgment in complex subsurface conditions means AI serves more as a powerful tool than a replacement, though productivity gains could reduce headcount over time.

ai_paths.reason: Overseeing complex infrastructure projects and managing technical site operations.
Monitoring environmental impact and ensuring regulatory compliance in field-work.
Utilizing specialized equipment for mineral and hydrocarbon resource exploration.
Evaluating workplace hazards and designing preventative safety measures.
Determining precise locations and boundaries for resource extraction sites.
Operating and maintaining large-scale industrial pressure and power systems.

education_detail: College students typically pursue a bachelor’sdegreeinengineering. High school students interested in studying petroleum engineering may benefit from taking classes in math, such as algebra, geometry, and calculus; and science, such as biology, chemistry, and physics. Bachelor’s degree engineering programs usually include academic, laboratory, and field work in subjects such as engineering principles, geology, and thermodynamics. College students may have an opportunity to participate in cooperative education programs or internships. Through partnerships with local businesses, these programs allow students to gain practical experience while they complete their education. Some colleges and universities offer a 5-year engineering program that leads to both a bachelor’s degree and a master’s degree. Some employers prefer to hire candidates who have a master's degree. Employers may prefer to hire candidates who graduated from an engineering program accredited by a professional association.
certifications: Licensure is not required for entry-level petroleum engineer positions. Experienced engineers may obtain a Professional Engineer (PE) license, which allows them to oversee the work of other engineers, sign off on projects, and provide services directly to the public. State licensure typically requires a bachelor’s or higher degree in engineering, a passing score on the Fundamentals of Engineering (FE) exam, several years of relevant work experience, and a passing score on the PE exam. Each state issues its own license. Most states recognize licensure from other states, as long as the licensing state’s requirements meet or exceed their own licensure requirements. Several states require continuing education for engineers to keep their licenses. Information on state licensing board requirements is available fromCareerOneStop.

title: Pharmacists
exposure_rationale: Pharmacists perform a mix of knowledge-intensive tasks (drug interaction checking, medication counseling, clinical decision support) and physical/interpersonal duties (dispensing medications, administering vaccinations, direct patient care). AI can significantly automate the information-processing aspects—drug interaction screening, prescription verification, inventory management, and clinical decision support—but the patient-facing, regulatory, and hands-on clinical roles create meaningful barriers. The profession is already seeing automation pressure in retail pharmacy (mail-order, automated dispensing), but expanding clinical roles in hospitals and clinics where human judgment and patient relationships are central will partially offset displacement.

ai_paths.reason: Expertise in patient care, anatomy, and clinical diagnostics.
Clinical knowledge of health conditions and patient rehabilitation.
Understanding of physiology and medical emergency response.
Technical precision and familiarity with healthcare environments.
Experience in patient education and navigating healthcare systems.
Knowledge of chemistry, biology, and sensitive client service.
Pharmacology knowledge and urgent medical decision-making skills.
Applying health science knowledge to improve client wellness.

education_detail: Pharmacists typically need a Doctor of Pharmacy (Pharm.D.) degree from an accredited pharmacy program. Admission requirements vary; however, Pharm.D. programs typically require applicants to have at least 2 years of prerequisite undergraduate courses in subjects such as anatomy and physiology, physics, and statistics. Some Pharm.D. programs require or prefer that applicants have a bachelor’s degree inbiology, ahealthcare and related, or aphysical sciencefield, such as chemistry. Pharm.D. programs usually take 4 years to finish, although some programs offer a 3-year option. Others admit high school graduates into a 6-year program. Pharm.D. programs include courses in sciences, pharmacology, and pharmacy law. Students also complete supervised work experiences, sometimes referred to as internships, in settings such as hospitals and retail pharmacies. Some pharmacy programs offer a dual-degree option. These programs allow students to get another graduate degree, such as a master’s degree in business administration (MBA) or a master’s degree in public health (MPH), along with their Pharm.D. degree.
certifications: All states require pharmacists to be licensed, although licensure requirements vary. After completing their degree, prospective pharmacists typically must pass two exams to get a license. Applicants also must complete a state-specified number of hours as an intern. To maintain licensure, pharmacists must complete continuing education. Information on state licensing board requirements is available fromCareerOneStop. In most states, pharmacists must be certified to administer vaccinations. Pharmacists may choose to earn a certification to show advanced knowledge in a specific field. For example, a pharmacist may earn certification in a specialty area, such as emergency care or oncology. Certifications from professional organizations generally require applicants to have work experience and pass an exam. Information on certifications is available fromCareerOneStop.

title: Pharmacy technicians
exposure_rationale: Pharmacy technicians perform a mix of physical tasks (measuring, packaging, labeling medications, operating dispensing equipment, making hospital rounds) and information-processing tasks (data entry, insurance claims, inventory management) that partially overlap with AI capabilities. Automated dispensing systems and AI-driven prescription management are already encroaching on some routine tasks, but the physical handling of medications, direct customer interaction, and regulatory oversight requirements create meaningful barriers. The projected 6% job growth suggests the field is not facing imminent displacement, likely because expanding healthcare demand and pharmacists shifting to patient care roles are offsetting automation pressures.
education_detail: Pharmacy technicians usually need a high school diploma or equivalent and typically learn their duties through on-the-job training. The training periods vary in length and subject matter according to the employer’s requirements. Other pharmacy technicians enter the occupation after completing postsecondary education programs in pharmacy technology. These programs are usually offered by vocational schools or community colleges. Most programs award a certificate after 1 year or less, although some programs last longer and lead to an associate’s degree. They cover a variety of subjects, such as arithmetic used in pharmacies, recordkeeping, ways of dispensing medications, and pharmacy law and ethics. Technicians also learn the names, uses, and doses of medications. Most programs also include clinical experience opportunities, in which students gain hands-on experience in a pharmacy.
certifications: Most states regulate pharmacy technicians in some way. Consult state Boards of Pharmacy for particular regulations. Requirements for pharmacy technicians in the states that regulate them typically include some or all of the following: High school diploma or GEDFormal education or training programExamFeesContinuing educationCriminal background check Some states and employers require pharmacy technicians to be certified. Even where it is not required, certification may make it easier to get a job. Many employers of pharmacy technicians will pay for employees to take the certification exam. Information on certifications is available fromCareerOneStop.

title: Phlebotomists
exposure_rationale: Phlebotomy is fundamentally a hands-on, physical job requiring manual dexterity to draw blood, direct patient interaction, and real-time human presence — none of which AI can replicate. The only digital components (entering sample information into a database, labeling) are minor and peripheral to the core work. AI has essentially no ability to automate the physical act of venipuncture or the interpersonal reassurance of anxious patients.
education_detail: Phlebotomists typically enter the occupation with a postsecondary nondegree award from a phlebotomy program. These programs are available from community colleges, vocational schools, or technical schools and usually take less than 1 year to complete. They involve instruction in anatomy, physiology, and medical terminology and laboratory work and lead to a certificate. Some employers hire candidates with a high school diploma and train them on the job. Whether through formal education or employer-provided training, the training that all phlebotomists receive includes instruction on how to identify, label, and track blood samples.
certifications: States may require that phlebotomists complete an accredited training program, have a license or certification, or meet other requirements. For specific requirements, contact your state licensing agency. Some employers prefer to hire phlebotomists who have earned professional certification, such as those offered by professional organizations. Requirements vary by organization but may include education and clinical experience, passing an exam, and practical components, such as drawing blood. Phlebotomists also may need to have Basic Life Support certification. Those who transport samples may need a driver’s license.

title: Photographers
exposure_rationale: Photography has significant physical and creative components requiring real-world presence, equipment operation, and interpersonal skills with clients, which limits direct AI automation of the core capture work. However, AI is already heavily impacting post-production (automated editing, enhancement, background replacement), stock photography demand (AI-generated images competing with stock photos), and commercial photography (AI image generation reducing need for product shoots). The combination of AI-generated imagery threatening demand, AI-assisted editing reducing post-production labor, and smartphone AI cameras eroding the entry-level market creates substantial indirect exposure, though the physical, relational, and artistic judgment aspects provide meaningful insulation.

ai_paths.reason: Expertise in visual storytelling and managing client experiences.
Strong eye for composition, color theory, and aesthetics.
Attention to detail and experience documenting physical conditions.
Skills in evidence documentation and keen situational awareness.

education_detail: Postsecondary education is not always required for photographers. However, many photographers take classes or earn a certificate or degree to improve their skills. Many universities, community colleges, vocational–technical institutes, and private trade and technical schools offer classes in photography. The subject matter in basic photography courses includes how to use various photography equipment, processes, and techniques. Art school training in photographic design and composition also may be useful. Some employers may require or prefer that candidates have a bachelor’sdegreein photography, photojournalism, or a relatedfine artsfield. Business, marketing, and accounting classes may be helpful for self-employed photographers.
certifications: Photographers who commercially operate drones, commonly known as unmanned aerial vehicles, must obtain certification from theFederal Aviation Administration(FAA). For specific guidelines and information, visit the FAA website’s section onunmanned aircraft systems.

title: Physical therapist assistants and aides
exposure_rationale: Physical therapist assistants and aides perform highly physical, hands-on work — manual therapy, massage, stretching, patient movement, and exercise guidance — that requires direct human presence and physical dexterity. AI has very limited ability to replicate the tactile, interpersonal, and physical components of this role. While AI might assist with scheduling, documentation, or treatment planning at the supervising PT level, the core daily tasks of PTAs and aides remain firmly in the physical world.
education_detail: All states require physical therapist assistants to have an associate’s degree from an accredited program. Programs typically last about 2 years and include coursework in subjects such as anatomy, physiology, and kinesiology. Assistants also gain hands-on experience during supervised clinical work. Physical therapist aides typically need a high school diploma or equivalent. They also usually need on-the-job training that can last from about one week to one month.
certifications: All states require physical therapist assistants to be licensed or certified. Licensure typically requires graduation from an accredited physical therapist assistant program and passing a qualifying exam. Some states require that applicants pass an exam on the state’s laws regulating the practice of physical therapy assistants, undergo a criminal background check, and be at least 18 years old. Physical therapist assistants also may need to take continuing education courses to keep their license. Check with your state board for specific licensing requirements. Information on state licensing board requirements is available fromCareerOneStop. Additionally, physical therapy assistants may earn certifications in cardiopulmonary resuscitation (CPR), basic life support (BLS), or other first-aid skills. Information on certifications is available fromCareerOneStop. States do not require physical therapist aides to be licensed.

industries.name: Offices of physical, occupational and speech therapists, and audiologists
Government, excluding state and local education and hospitals

title: Physical therapists
exposure_rationale: Physical therapy is fundamentally a hands-on, physical profession requiring direct patient contact, manual therapy techniques, and real-time observation of movement and function that cannot be replicated digitally. AI can assist with administrative tasks, documentation, treatment planning suggestions, and analyzing patient progress data, but the core work — physically manipulating joints, guiding exercises, and providing tactile feedback — requires human presence. The strong projected job growth (11%) reflects that demand is driven by demographic trends rather than being threatened by automation.
education_detail: Physical therapists need a Doctor of Physical Therapy (DPT) degree from an accredited program. DPT programs typically last 3 years. Physical therapy programs typically require a bachelor'sdegree, which may be inrecreation and fitnessorhealthcare and relatedfields, and prerequisite courses such as anatomy, chemistry, and physics. Physical therapist programs often include courses in biomechanics, neuroscience, and pharmacology. Physical therapist students also complete clinical work, during which they gain supervised experience in areas such as acute care and orthopedic care. Physical therapists may apply to a clinical residency program after graduation. Residencies typically last about 1 year and provide additional training and experience in specialty areas of care. Physical therapists who have completed a residency program may choose to specialize further by participating in a fellowship in an advanced clinical area.
certifications: All states require physical therapists to be licensed, which includes passing a qualifying exam. Other requirements vary by state. For example, some states also require a law exam and a criminal background check. Continuing education is typically required for physical therapists to keep their license. Check with your state board for specific licensing requirements. Information on state licensing board requirements is available fromCareerOneStop. After gaining work experience, some physical therapists choose to become a board-certified specialist. Certification is available in clinical specialty areas of physical therapy, such as orthopedics, sports, and geriatrics. Board specialist certification requires passing an exam and completing clinical work in the specialty area. Information on certifications is available fromCareerOneStop.

industries.name: Offices of physical, occupational and speech therapists, and audiologists

title: Physician assistants
exposure_rationale: Physician assistants perform substantial knowledge work (reviewing histories, interpreting diagnostics, documentation) where AI can meaningfully assist, but the core of the job requires physical patient examination, hands-on procedures like suturing and setting bones, and real-time clinical judgment in unpredictable medical situations. AI tools may improve efficiency in documentation, differential diagnosis support, and treatment research, but cannot replace the physical presence and procedural skills central to the role. The strong projected job growth (20%) further suggests AI is more likely to augment than displace PAs in the near term.
education_detail: Applicants to physician assistant education programs typically have a bachelor’sdegreeand some experience with patient care. Although programs vary, most require applicants to have taken undergraduate coursework with a focus in science. Bachelor's degrees are often inhealthcareor a related field, such asbiology. Programs also may require that applicants have experience as amedical assistant,EMT or paramedic, or another occupation that involves patient care. Physician assistant education programs usually take at least 2 years of postbaccalaureate study. Physician assistant education includes classroom and laboratory instruction in subjects such as human anatomy, clinical medicine, and pharmacology. The programs also include supervised clinical training in several specialties, such as family medicine, internal medicine, and emergency medicine.
certifications: All states and the District of Columbia require physician assistants to be licensed. To become licensed, candidates must pass a qualifying exam. A physician assistant who passes the exam may use the credential “Physician Assistant-Certified (PA-C).” Information on state licensing board requirements is available fromCareerOneStop. To maintain their PA-C certification, physician assistants must complete continuing education and pass a recertification exam within a specified number of years. In most states, laws require physician assistants to hold an agreement with a supervising physician. Although the physician does not need to be onsite at all times, collaboration between physicians and physician assistants is required for practice. Physician assistant positions may require basic life support (BLS) certification. Information on certifications is available fromCareerOneStop.

industries.name: Government, excluding state and local education and hospitals
Educational services; state, local, and private

title: Physicians and surgeons
exposure_rationale: Physicians and surgeons have significant physical, hands-on components (surgery, physical exams, procedures) that AI cannot replace, but substantial portions of their work — interpreting diagnostic images, analyzing test results, documentation, treatment planning, and pattern recognition in diagnosis — are highly amenable to AI augmentation. Some specialties like radiology and pathology face higher exposure as their core work is image/data interpretation, while surgeons and emergency physicians face lower exposure due to irreplaceable manual dexterity and real-time physical judgment. AI will likely make physicians more productive and may reduce demand at the margins (especially in diagnostic roles), but regulatory, liability, and patient-trust factors will slow displacement significantly.
education_detail: In addition to requiring a bachelor’s degree, physicians and surgeons typically need either a Medical Doctor (M.D.) or a Doctor of Osteopathic Medicine (D.O.) degree. No specific undergraduatedegreeis required to enter an M.D. or D.O. program, but applicants to medical school usually have studied subjects such asbiology,physical science, orhealthcare and relatedfields. Medical schools are highly competitive. Applicants usually must submit transcripts, scores from the Medical College Admission Test (MCAT), and letters of recommendation. Medical schools also consider an applicant’s personality, leadership qualities, and participation in extracurricular activities. Most schools require applicants to interview with members of the admissions committee. Some medical schools offer combined undergraduate and medical school programs that last 6 to 8 years. Schools may also offer combined graduate degrees, such as M.D.-Ph.D., M.D.-MBA, and M.D.-MPH. Students spend the first phase of medical school in classrooms, small groups, and laboratories, taking courses such as anatomy, biochemistry, pharmacology, psychology, medical ethics, and in the laws governing medicine. They also gain practical skills: learning to take medical histories, examine patients, and diagnose illnesses. During their second phase of medical school, students work with patients under the supervision of experienced physicians in hospitals and clinics. They gain experience in diagnosing and treating illnesses through clerkships, or rotations, in a variety of areas, including internal medicine, pediatrics, and surgery.
certifications: All states require physicians and surgeons to be licensed; requirements vary by state. To qualify for a license, candidates must graduate from an accredited medical school and complete residency training in their specialty. Information on state licensing board requirements is available fromCareerOneStop. Licensure requirements include passing standardized national exams. For specific state information about licensing, contact your state’s medical board. Board certification in a specialty is not required for physicians and surgeons; however, it may increase their employment opportunities. M.D.s and D.O.s seeking board certification in a specialty may spend up to 9 years in residency training; the length of time varies with the specialty. To become board certified, candidates must complete a residency program and pass a specialty certification exam from a medical certifying board. Information on certifications is available fromCareerOneStop.

title: Physicists and astronomers
exposure_rationale: Physicists and astronomers do substantial knowledge work — mathematical modeling, data analysis, writing papers and proposals, developing software — all of which AI can meaningfully assist with, and AI tools like large language models and neural networks are already being used in physics research (e.g., protein folding, particle physics data analysis, gravitational wave detection). However, the core of the job involves deep creative scientific reasoning, designing novel experiments, and operating specialized physical equipment, which remain highly resistant to AI automation. The occupation is likely to see significant productivity gains from AI assistance rather than wholesale displacement, keeping exposure in the high-but-not-extreme range.

ai_paths.reason: Application of radiation physics and monitoring equipment expertise.
Transfer of celestial mechanics and propulsion system knowledge.
Precision measurement and high-end scientific instrument maintenance.
Applying analytical laboratory methods and data collection protocols.
Troubleshooting complex electronic and imaging hardware systems.
Integration of physical principles with automated mechanical systems.
Expertise in coordinate systems, optics, and spatial mathematics.

education_detail: A Ph.D. in physics, astronomy, or a related field is typically required for jobs in research or academia. Graduate students may concentrate in a subfield of physics or astronomy, such as condensed matter physics or cosmology. In addition to coursework in physics or astronomy, Ph.D. students need to take courses in math, such as calculus, linear algebra, and statistics. Computer science also may be useful for developing programs to gather, analyze, and model data. A bachelor’sdegreeinphysical scienceor a related field, such asengineering, usually is required to enter a graduate program in physics or astronomy. Undergraduate physics programs typically include courses such as quantum mechanics, thermodynamics, and electromagnetism. Jobseekers with a bachelor’s degree in physics usually are qualified to work as technicians and research assistants in related fields, such as engineering and computer science. Those with a bachelor’s degree in astronomy also may qualify to work as an assistant at an observatory. Students who do not want to continue their studies to the doctoral level may want to take courses in instrument building and computer science. Master’s degree and bachelor’s degree holders may be eligible for jobs in the federal government. Others may become science teachers inmiddle schoolsorhigh schools.
certifications: Some positions with the federal government, such as those involving nuclear energy, may require applicants to be U.S. citizens and hold a security clearance.

industries.name: Research and development in the physical, engineering, and life sciences
Colleges, universities, and professional schools; state

title: Plumbers, pipefitters, and steamfitters
exposure_rationale: Plumbing, pipefitting, and steamfitting are fundamentally physical trades requiring hands-on work in varied, unpredictable environments — installing pipes, troubleshooting leaks, working in tight spaces, and lifting heavy materials. AI cannot perform these physical tasks and has minimal impact on the core job. AI might marginally assist with cost estimation, blueprint reading, or scheduling, but these peripheral tasks represent a small fraction of the work.
education_detail: A high school diploma or equivalent is typically required to become a plumber, pipefitter, or steamfitter. Vocational-technical schools offer courses in pipe system design, safety, and tool use. They also offer welding courses that are required by some pipefitter and steamfitter apprenticeship training programs.
certifications: Most states and some localities require plumbers to be licensed. Although licensing requirements vary, states and localities often require workers to have 2 to 5 years of experience and to pass an exam that shows their knowledge of the trade before allowing plumbers to work independently. Plumbers may also obtain optional certification, such as in plumbing design, to broaden career opportunities. In addition, most employers require plumbers to have a driver’s license. Some states require pipefitters and steamfitters to be licensed; they may also require a special license to work on gas lines. Licensing typically requires an exam or work experience or both. Contact your state’s licensing board for more information.

industries.name: Government, excluding state and local education and hospitals

title: Podiatrists
exposure_rationale: Podiatry is fundamentally a hands-on medical profession requiring physical examination, surgical procedures, and direct patient care that cannot be replicated by AI. AI may assist with diagnostic image analysis, medical record review, or treatment planning support, but the core work — performing surgery, physical assessments, and patient interaction — requires physical presence and manual dexterity. The occupation has low AI exposure similar to other procedural medical specialties where the physical component dominates.

ai_paths.reason: Familiarity with sterile environments and surgical instruments for procedures.
Deep understanding of soft tissue manipulation and foot reflexology.

education_detail: Podiatrists must have a Doctor of Podiatric Medicine (DPM) degree from an accredited college of podiatric medicine. A DPM degree program takes 4 years to complete. Admission to podiatric medicine programs requires at least 3 years of undergraduate education, but nearly all prospective students have a bachelor’sdegreeinhealthcare,biology, orphysical science. Although programs might not specify the undergraduate degree required for admission, applicants must have completed courses in laboratory sciences such as biology, chemistry, and physics, as well as general coursework in subjects such as English. Applicants to DPM schools usually submit scores from the Medical College Admission Test (MCAT) and letters of recommendation. They also may indicate that they shadowed a podiatrist. Courses for a DPM degree are similar to those for other medical degrees. They include anatomy, physiology, pharmacology, and pathology. Podiatric medical students gain supervised experience by completing clinical rotations while in school.
certifications: Podiatrists in every state must be licensed. Podiatrists must pay a fee and pass all parts of a qualifying exam. Some states have additional requirements. Information on state licensing board requirements is available fromCareerOneStop. Many podiatrists choose to become board certified. Certification generally requires a combination of work experience and passing an exam. Information on certifications is available fromCareerOneStop.

title: Police and detectives
exposure_rationale: Police and detective work is predominantly physical and interpersonal — patrolling, making arrests, responding to emergencies, and interacting with the public in unpredictable real-world environments. AI can assist with peripheral tasks like report writing, database searches, and case analysis, but the core job requires physical presence, human judgment in dynamic situations, and legal authority that cannot be delegated to AI. The occupation's strong institutional, legal, and public-trust requirements further insulate it from significant AI-driven restructuring.
education_detail: Police and detective applicants must have at least a high school diploma or equivalent, although some federal agencies and police departments may require that applicants have completed college coursework or a college degree. Many community colleges and 4-year colleges and universities offer programs in law enforcement and criminal justice. Knowledge of a foreign language is an asset in many federal agencies and geographical regions. Fish and game wardens typically need a bachelor’s degree; desirable fields of study include wildlife science,biology, ornatural resources. Federal Wildlife Officers and some state-level fish and game wardens typically do not need a bachelor’s degree. Federal agencies such as theFederal Bureau of Investigationmay require prospective detectives and investigators to have a bachelor's degree. Many applicants for entry-level police jobs have completed some college coursework, and a significant number are college graduates. Commonfields of degreeincludesecurity and protective serviceandsocial science.

title: Political scientists
exposure_rationale: Political science work is predominantly knowledge-based — researching, analyzing data, writing reports, forecasting trends, and synthesizing information from documents and surveys — all tasks where AI is rapidly becoming capable. AI tools can already assist substantially with literature reviews, data analysis, policy evaluation, and drafting reports, making each political scientist significantly more productive and potentially reducing headcount. However, the job retains meaningful human elements such as expert judgment, stakeholder relationships, navigating political nuance, and credibility in presenting findings to government clients, which tempers the exposure somewhat.
education_detail: Political scientists typically need a master’s degree to enter the occupation. Relevant master’s degree programs include fields such as political science, public administration, and public policy. These programs often have courses in other disciplines, such as psychology and economics, as well as in government, policy, and politics. Employers may consider candidates who have a bachelor’sdegreein political science for certain entry-level positions, such as with political campaigns or in government. Some employers prefer that political scientist candidates have a Ph.D., which requires several years of study beyond a bachelor’s degree. Prospective political scientists may benefit from participating in internship or volunteer opportunities while in school. These experiences may offer students a chance to develop the analytical, research, and other skills they need to be successful.

industries.name: Educational services; state, local, and private
Religious, grantmaking, civic, professional, and similar organizations

title: Postal service workers
exposure_rationale: Postal service work is predominantly physical — carrying, sorting, and delivering mail requires human presence and physical stamina — which limits direct AI automation of the core job. However, AI-driven sorting technology, route optimization, and reduced mail volume from digital communications are already displacing workers indirectly, as reflected in the projected employment decline. The sorting/processing sub-role has higher exposure to automation than mail carriers, but the physical delivery component creates a meaningful barrier to full AI displacement.
education_detail: Although no formal educational credential is typically required to enter these occupations, most postal service workers have at least a high school diploma or the equivalent.
certifications: Postal service workers who operate a motor vehicle need a driver's license. In addition, mail carriers must have a safe driving record and pass a road test before driving on the job.

title: Postsecondary education administrators
exposure_rationale: Postsecondary education administrators perform a mix of knowledge work (data analysis, record management, policy development, application review) and interpersonal/relationship-driven work (advising students, building faculty relationships, campus presence). AI can meaningfully automate or assist with data analysis, application screening, scheduling, record-keeping, and communications, but the core of the role involves human judgment, institutional relationships, and physical campus presence. The occupation sits at a moderate-to-high exposure level where AI tools will enhance productivity and may reduce headcount at the margins, but the relational and institutional leadership dimensions create meaningful barriers to full automation.

ai_paths.reason: Utilizes administrative management and educational program supervision.
Pivots facilities oversight and project coordination experience.
Leverages case management and community resource navigation.
Uses curriculum knowledge to lead vocational training.

education_detail: Postsecondary education administrators typically need a master’s degree. However, a bachelor’s degree may be sufficient for positions at small colleges and universities. Degrees may be in a variety offields, such aseducation,business, orsocial science. Provosts and deans often must have a Ph.D. Some begin their careers as professors and later move into administration. They have a doctorate in the field in which they taught or in higher education.

industries.name: Colleges, universities, and professional schools; private
Colleges, universities, and professional schools; state

title: Postsecondary teachers
exposure_rationale: Postsecondary teachers have significant knowledge-work components — course design, grading, research, writing — where AI can meaningfully assist or partially automate, but the core job involves in-person instruction, mentorship, and human relationships that create real barriers to full AI substitution. AI can help with lecture preparation, grading, and research synthesis, potentially making each professor more productive, but the credential-signaling value of human instruction and the interpersonal dynamics of teaching limit displacement. The occupation sits at a moderate-to-high exposure level: AI will reshape how professors work substantially, but the physical presence requirement and the institutional/social value placed on human educators provide meaningful protection.

ai_paths.reason: Adapting complex curriculum for diverse individual learning needs.
Mentoring individuals and analyzing performance to improve outcomes.
Applying academic expertise to practical, hands-on vocational instruction.
Designing structured programs to facilitate personal development and learning.
Leading teams, managing schedules, and explaining technical specifications.
Overseeing educational standards and managing departmental administrative tasks.
Communicating complex information to educate and support specific populations.
Advocating for students and navigating complex institutional systems.

education_detail: Postsecondary teachers who work for 4-year colleges and universities typically need a Ph.D. or other doctorate in theirfield of degree. For some specialties or for part-time positions, schools may hire those with a master’s degree or who are doctoral degree candidates. Doctoral programs usually take several years to complete, and students typically need a bachelor’s or master’s degree to enroll. Most Ph.D. programs require students to write a doctoral dissertation, a paper presenting original research in their field of study, which they then defend in questioning from experts. Candidates usually specialize in a subfield, such as organic chemistry or European history. Community colleges may hire those with a master’s degree. However, some institutions prefer that applicants have a Ph.D.
certifications: Postsecondary teachers who prepare students for an occupation that requires a license, certification, or registration, may need to have—or may benefit from having—the same credential. For example, a postsecondary nursing teacher might need a nursing license or a postsecondary education teacher might need a teaching license.

title: Power plant operators, distributors, and dispatchers
exposure_rationale: Power plant operators work in a hybrid environment — much of their work involves monitoring digital control systems, reading data, and making decisions based on information flows, which AI can increasingly assist with or automate. However, the job also requires physical presence, hands-on equipment checks, emergency response, and operation of complex physical infrastructure in safety-critical environments. The BLS already projects a 10% employment decline driven by automation and smart-grid technology, confirming meaningful AI/automation exposure, but the physical presence requirements, safety-critical judgment, and regulatory licensing constraints (especially for nuclear operators) create significant barriers to full AI displacement.
education_detail: Power plant operators, distributors, and dispatchers typically need at least a high school diploma or the equivalent to enter the occupation. However, employers may prefer to hire candidates who have a postsecondary degree, such as from a college or vocational school. Employers generally look for candidates who have a strong background in science and mathematics, especially algebra and trigonometry.
certifications: Nuclear power reactor operators must be licensed through the NRC. They typically begin working as unlicensed operators in nuclear power plants, where they gain the required knowledge and experience to start the licensing process. To become licensed, operators must meet training and experience requirements, pass a medical exam, and pass the NRC licensing exam. To keep their license, operators must pass annual and periodic exams. Licenses are not transferrable between plants, so an operator must get a new license to work in another facility. Some power plant operators may be licensed by state licensing boards as stationary engineers, boiler operators, or firefighters. Requirements vary by state and depend on the specific job functions that the operator performs. Information on state licensing board requirements is available fromCareerOneStop. Power plant operators, distributors, and dispatchers who are in positions that could affect the power grid may need to be certified. Information on certifications is available fromCareerOneStop.

title: Preschool and childcare center directors
exposure_rationale: This role combines significant administrative and knowledge work (budgeting, policy development, curriculum planning, record-keeping) with substantial physical presence requirements — supervising staff in classrooms, meeting with parents and children, and ensuring regulatory compliance on-site. AI can meaningfully assist with the administrative, communication, and documentation tasks, but the core of the job involves interpersonal leadership, relationship-building with families and staff, and physical oversight of a childcare environment that cannot be replicated digitally. The human judgment and trust required in early childhood settings creates a natural ceiling on AI displacement.
education_detail: Most states require preschool and childcare center directors to have at least a bachelor’s degree, but educational requirements vary by state. Employers may prefer candidates who have adegree, or at least some postsecondary coursework, in early childhoodeducation. These programs teach child development, provide strategies for instructing young children, and show how to observe and document children’s progress.
certifications: States may require childcare centers, including those in private homes, to be licensed. To qualify for licensure, staff must pass a background check and meet a minimum training requirement. Some states have more requirements, such as requiring staff to have certifications in cardiopulmonary resuscitation (CPR) and first aid. Some employers have additional requirements such as professional credentials. Candidates may need to pay a fee, take coursework, obtain experience in the field, and be observed while working with children. Information on certifications is available fromCareerOneStop.

industries.name: Religious, grantmaking, civic, professional, and similar organizations

title: Preschool teachers
exposure_rationale: Preschool teaching is fundamentally a physical, in-person occupation centered on caregiving, emotional support, and hands-on interaction with very young children — tasks that AI cannot meaningfully replicate. While AI might assist with minor administrative tasks like record-keeping or lesson planning, the core job requires constant physical presence, real-time responsiveness to children's emotional and developmental needs, and human relationship-building with both children and parents. The work is inherently embodied and interpersonal, creating a strong natural barrier to AI displacement.
education_detail: Preschool teachers typically need at least an associate’s degree. Preschool teachers in center-based Head Start programs are required to have at least an associate’s degree. However, at least 50 percent of all preschool teachers in Head Start programs nationwide must have a bachelor’s degree in early childhoodeducationor a relatedfield. Those with a degree in a related field, such aspsychology, must have experience teaching preschool-age children. In public schools, preschool teachers are generally required to have at least a bachelor’s degree in early childhood education or a related field. Bachelor’s degree programs include instruction on children’s development, teaching young children, and observing and documenting children’s progress.
certifications: In public schools, preschool teachers must be licensed to teach early childhood education, which covers preschool through third grade. Requirements vary by state, but they generally require a bachelor’s degree and passing an exam to demonstrate competency. Most states require teachers to complete continuing education credits in order to maintain their license. Some states require preschool teachers to obtain a professional credential. Obtaining this credential may require coursework, experience in the field, a written exam, and observation of the candidate working with children.

title: Private detectives and investigators
exposure_rationale: Private investigation is a hybrid occupation combining significant digital/research work (searching records, analyzing online data, writing reports) with substantial physical components (surveillance, field interviews, undercover work) that AI cannot easily replicate. AI tools can dramatically accelerate the digital research and background-check portions of the job, potentially making investigators more productive with fewer needed for routine tasks. However, physical surveillance, in-person interviews, real-time judgment in the field, and the legal/ethical navigation of evidence collection remain human-dependent activities that create a meaningful barrier to full AI displacement.
education_detail: Education requirements vary. Some employers require a high school diploma or the equivalent, while others require a bachelor’sdegree. Fields of study may include those that are closely related, such assecurity and protective service, or those that have wide applicability, such asbusiness.
certifications: Most states require private detectives and investigators to be licensed. Check with your state and locality for information about age, experience, and other requirements for licensing. Depending on the position, private detectives and investigators may need a driver’s license. Some workers opt to earn professional certification, which demonstrates competence in a particular area of investigation work.

industries.name: Government, excluding state and local education and hospitals

title: Probation officers and correctional treatment specialists
exposure_rationale: This occupation combines significant knowledge work (writing reports, case assessments, rehabilitation planning) with substantial in-person, physical components (home visits, court appearances, drug testing, community supervision) that AI cannot easily replace. AI could assist with report writing, risk assessment tools, and case documentation, but the core of the job — building relationships with challenging clients, conducting field visits, making nuanced human judgments about safety and rehabilitation — requires physical presence and interpersonal skills. The legal and institutional context also creates barriers to AI automation, as decisions carry significant consequences requiring human accountability.

ai_paths.reason: Navigating complex systems to improve individual and community outcomes.
Developing treatment plans and facilitating behavioral rehabilitation programs.

education_detail: Probation officers and correctional treatment specialists typically need a bachelor's degree. Commonfields of degreeinclude criminal justice or a relatedsecurity and protective servicefield,social science, orpsychology. Requirements vary by jurisdiction.

title: Producers and directors
exposure_rationale: Producers and directors do substantial knowledge work — script selection, budgeting, scheduling, communication, and post-production oversight — that AI can increasingly assist with, but the core of the job involves real-time human coordination, creative judgment, and on-set physical presence that AI cannot replicate. AI tools can help with scriptwriting, editing, budgeting, and even casting research, making individual producers/directors more productive, but the relational and leadership dimensions (managing talent, negotiating, inspiring creative teams) remain deeply human. The job is a mix of digital knowledge work and physical/interpersonal coordination, placing it in the high-exposure range but not at the top.

ai_paths.reason: Directly transfers complex project oversight and budget management skills.
Applies industry knowledge to facility and operations management.
Utilizes leadership, scheduling, and creative programming expertise.

education_detail: Producers and directors typically need a bachelor'sdegreein film or cinema studies or a related field, such as arts management,business,communications technology, or theater. In film or cinema studies programs, students learn about film history, editing, screenwriting, cinematography, and the filmmaking process. Stage directors may complete a degree in theater, and some go on to earn a Master of Fine Arts (MFA) degree. Courses may include directing, playwriting, set design, and acting.

title: Project management specialists
exposure_rationale: Project management is predominantly knowledge work — planning, scheduling, budgeting, document production, and communication — all of which are domains where AI is rapidly improving and already useful. AI tools can automate status reporting, risk identification, resource scheduling, and document generation, making individual PMs significantly more productive and potentially reducing headcount needed per project. However, the role retains meaningful human elements: building client trust, navigating interpersonal team dynamics, exercising judgment in ambiguous situations, and sometimes being physically present at project sites, which tempers the exposure somewhat.

ai_paths.reason: Directly transfers scheduling, budgeting, and oversight to physical sites.
Leverages organizational oversight and facility coordination expertise.

education_detail: To enter the occupation, project management specialists typically need a bachelor’sdegreeinbusiness, project management, or a related field. Some employers prefer to hire candidates who have a degree in a technical field related to the industry in which they will work, such ascomputer and information technologyorengineering.
certifications: Although not always required, professional certification demonstrates competency to prospective clients and employers. Information on certifications is available fromCareerOneStop.

title: Property appraisers and assessors
exposure_rationale: Property appraisal involves a meaningful mix of physical site visits and inspection work alongside substantial knowledge work (data analysis, comparable sales analysis, report writing) that AI can increasingly automate. Automated Valuation Models (AVMs) are already disrupting routine residential appraisals, and AI can assist heavily with data gathering, comparables analysis, and report generation. However, the physical inspection component, local market judgment, regulatory requirements for licensed human sign-off, and the need to defend assessments in hearings provide meaningful barriers to full automation.

ai_paths.reason: Expertise in property structures, building costs, and site evaluation.
Experience with legal descriptions, boundaries, and land measurements.
Deep knowledge of building codes and structural integrity assessment.
Detail-oriented site examination and risk assessment skills transfer well.
Experience conducting thorough physical inspections and documenting findings.
Utilizes land use analysis and environmental assessment capabilities.

education_detail: Although requirements vary, property appraisers and assessors typically need a bachelor’s degree. Commonfields of degreeincludebusiness,social science, andpsychology. College courses in subjects such as computer science, finance, and business or real estate law may be useful for prospective appraisers and assessors. Most states set education and experience requirements that assessors must meet in order to practice. A few states have no statewide requirements; instead, each locality sets the standards. In some localities, candidates may qualify with a high school diploma.
certifications: Federal law requires appraisers of real estate to have a state license or certification when working on federally related transactions, such as appraisals for loans made by federally insured banks and financial institutions. There is no such federal requirement for appraisers of personal and business property or for assessors, although some states require certification. Information on state licensing board requirements is available fromCareerOneStop.

title: Property, real estate, and community association managers
exposure_rationale: Property management involves a significant mix of physical/interpersonal tasks (inspecting properties, meeting tenants, resolving disputes, showing units) and knowledge work (financial reporting, budgeting, lease administration, compliance). AI can meaningfully automate the administrative and financial components—rent collection tracking, vacancy posting, maintenance request routing, financial reporting—but the core relationship management, physical inspections, and on-site problem resolution require human presence. The BLS itself notes that automation of some tasks may slow employment growth, suggesting moderate but not transformative AI impact.

ai_paths.reason: Direct experience overseeing building systems and facility upkeep.
Familiarity with property maintenance and common structural issues.
Experience navigating community resources and resident advocacy.

education_detail: A high school diploma or equivalent is typically required for onsite property management positions. Employers may prefer to hire college graduates for positions in commercial management positions related to overseeing a property’s finances or contracts.Fields of degreefor bachelor’s or master’s study may include business administration, accounting, finance, real estate, or public administration.
certifications: Property, real estate, and community association managers may need a license issued by the state in which they work. In most states, property managers must have a property management license or real estate broker’s license. Real estate managers who buy or sell property must have a real estate license in the state in which they practice. In some states, community association managers also need a real estate license. Managers of public housing subsidized by the federal government must hold certifications. Many states require property, real estate, and community association managers to obtain professional credentials or licensure. Requirements vary by state, but managers working in states without requirements may still obtain designations to show competence and professionalism. Most states require recertification. For more information, contact your state licensing agency. Information on state licensing board requirements is available fromCareerOneStop.

title: Psychiatric technicians and aides
exposure_rationale: This occupation is fundamentally physical and interpersonal — monitoring patients, assisting with daily living activities, restraining violent patients, and providing hands-on therapeutic care in clinical settings. AI cannot replicate the physical presence, human empathy, and real-time behavioral observation required. While AI might assist with documentation or scheduling at the margins, the core work is irreducibly human and physical, and the occupation is actually projected to grow significantly due to rising demand for mental health services.
education_detail: Psychiatric technicians typically need a postsecondary certificate for psychiatric technicians, behavioral health technicians, or similar titles. Programs for these certificates or associate’s degrees, available at community colleges and technical schools, train students in basic nursing skills. They include courses in psychology, anatomy, and pharmacology and also may include supervised clinical work experience. Some employers prefer to hire candidates who have a bachelor’sdegree. Psychiatric aides typically need a high school diploma or equivalent.
certifications: Some states require psychiatric technicians to have a license. Requirements may include completing an accredited education program and passing an exam. Contact your state licensing board for additional information. Information on state licensing board requirements is available fromCareerOneStop. Psychiatric technicians may choose to earn optional certification. Requirements vary based on education and work experience. Employers may require candidates to have certification in cardiopulmonary resuscitation (CPR) or basic life support (BLS). Information on certifications is available fromCareerOneStop.

title: Psychologists
exposure_rationale: Psychology involves substantial human interaction, therapeutic relationships, and real-time emotional attunement that AI cannot replicate — the therapeutic alliance itself is a core mechanism of treatment efficacy. However, AI can meaningfully assist with report writing, psychological assessment scoring and interpretation, research analysis, and administrative tasks, making practitioners more productive. The profession's strong licensing requirements, ethical constraints around client confidentiality, and the irreplaceable value of human empathy and judgment in clinical settings create significant barriers to deeper AI displacement.

ai_paths.reason: Transfers knowledge of cognitive and emotional health to rehabilitation.
Leverages interpersonal communication and health advocacy expertise.
Combines psychological motivation techniques with physical healthcare.
Applies patient care and mental health monitoring skills.

education_detail: Psychologists typically need at least a master’s degree to enter the occupation. Applicants to graduate-level psychology programs typically have a bachelor's degree in afieldsuch aspsychology,education, orsocial science. Clinical and counseling psychologists typically need a Doctor of Philosophy (Ph.D.) in psychology or a Doctor of Psychology (Psy.D.) degree. Ph.D. programs usually emphasize research that culminates in a dissertation, while Psy.D. programs focus on applying psychological principles to working with clients or patients. As part of either program, students are typically required to complete an internship. Industrial–organizational psychologists typically need a master’s degree. Programs for these psychologists usually include courses in statistics, research design, and topics specific to understanding the relationships between people and workplaces. School psychologists typically need at least a master’s degree in school psychology. Other degrees for school psychologists include education specialist (Ed.S.) and doctoral (Ph.D. or Psy.D.) degrees. School psychologist programs include coursework in education and psychology to address both of these components in students’ development. These programs also usually require completion of an internship.
certifications: Most states require licensure for those who provide psychology services or use the title “psychologist.” All states and the District of Columbia require that psychologists who practice independently be licensed where they work. Licensing laws vary by state and by type of position. For more information, contact your state’s licensing board. Clinical and counseling psychologists are licensed in every state. School psychologists also typically need a credential, such as a license or certification from their state’s board of education. Licensure typically requires applicants to have earned a master’s or doctoral degree and completed supervised experience. They also may need to have passed an exam. All states require psychologists to complete continuing education to maintain licensure. Information on state licensing board requirements is available fromCareerOneStop. Certification, which may be optional or may be required by employers, is available from professional associations. Information on certifications is available fromCareerOneStop.

title: Public relations and fundraising managers
exposure_rationale: PR and fundraising managers do substantial knowledge work — writing press releases, crafting messaging, analyzing trends, and planning campaigns — where AI can meaningfully boost productivity. However, the core of the job involves relationship-building, face-to-face donor meetings, managing reputational crises with human judgment, and leading teams, which are inherently human activities that AI cannot replicate. The managerial and interpersonal dimensions create a natural ceiling on AI displacement, placing this occupation in the high-exposure but not very-high-exposure range.

ai_paths.reason: Applying high-level project oversight and stakeholder coordination skills.
Leveraging event planning expertise and public engagement strategies.
Utilizing leadership, budgeting, and community relationship management.
Transferring interpersonal communication and program development abilities.
Applying talent evaluation and motivational communication techniques.
Managing operations, staff, and customer-facing brand reputation.
Using outreach and advocacy skills to support populations.

education_detail: For public relations and fundraising management positions, a bachelor’sdegreein a field such as public relations,communications, orbusinesstypically is required. However, some employers prefer to hire candidates who have a master’s degree, particularly in public relations, journalism, fundraising, or nonprofit management. Courses in advertising, business administration, public affairs, public speaking, and creative and technical writing can be helpful.
certifications: Although not mandatory, public relations managers can become certified. Candidates qualify based on a combination of experience and education and must pass an exam to become certified. Fundraisers who are awarded certification demonstrate a level of professional competency to prospective employers. To become certified, candidates must meet certain education, professional practice, and professional performance requirements, as well as pass an exam. Fundraisers must apply for renewal every 3 years to keep their certification valid. Information on certifications is available fromCareerOneStop.

title: Public relations specialists
exposure_rationale: Public relations work is predominantly knowledge-based and digital — writing press releases, drafting speeches, managing social media, and crafting messaging — all tasks where AI is already highly capable and improving rapidly. However, the job also requires significant interpersonal skills, relationship-building with media contacts, strategic judgment in sensitive situations, and representing clients in public-facing contexts, which creates meaningful barriers to full automation. AI tools can dramatically boost individual PR specialist productivity (drafting content, monitoring sentiment, analyzing coverage), which may moderate employment growth even as demand for the function persists.
education_detail: Public relations specialists typically need a bachelor’sdegreein public relations or anothercommunicationsfield,social science, orbusiness. Through such programs, students may produce a portfolio of work that demonstrates their ability to prospective employers. Although it is not typically required to enter the occupation, professional certification is preferred by some employers hiring candidates for public relations specialist jobs.

title: Public safety telecommunicators
exposure_rationale: Public safety telecommunicators work primarily at computer workstations handling calls, logging information, and coordinating dispatch — all digital tasks where AI is advancing rapidly (voice recognition, automated triage, computer-aided dispatch optimization). However, the job requires real-time human judgment in high-stakes, emotionally volatile situations, empathy with distressed callers, and accountability that creates strong resistance to full automation. AI can meaningfully augment this role (auto-transcription, priority scoring, protocol guidance) but the liability, unpredictability, and human trust requirements make complete displacement unlikely in the near term.
education_detail: Public safety telecommunicators typically need a high school diploma to enter the occupation.
certifications: Many states and localities require public safety telecommunicators to be certified. One certification is the Emergency Medical Dispatcher (EMD) certification, which enables dispatchers to give medical assistance over the phone. Public safety telecommunicators may choose to pursue additional certifications, which may help to demonstrate their leadership skills and knowledge. Information on certifications is available fromCareerOneStop.

title: Purchasing managers, buyers, and purchasing agents
exposure_rationale: Purchasing work is predominantly knowledge-based — analyzing price proposals, evaluating suppliers, maintaining records, and managing contracts — all of which are tasks AI can increasingly automate or significantly accelerate. The BLS itself notes that organizations will likely continue to automate procurement tasks and may use AI, which may limit growth. However, the role retains meaningful human elements such as supplier relationship management, negotiation, site visits, and judgment calls on quality and ethics that provide some buffer against full automation.

ai_paths.reason: Applying contract negotiation and material cost control skills.
Utilizing budgeting, vendor contracting, and operational oversight.
Managing service contracts and purchasing for facility maintenance.

education_detail: Purchasing managers, buyers, and purchasing agents typically need a bachelor'sdegreefor entry-level positions. However, a high school diploma or the equivalent may be sufficient for some positions. Fields of study vary, but a degree inbusiness, finance, or supply management may be helpful. For positions as a buyer or purchasing agent of farm products, a degree inagriculture, agriculture production, or animal science may be beneficial.
certifications: Some workers may choose to earn optional certification in an area such as procurement or supply chain management. Certification helps to demonstrate that candidates have attained competency in the field.
