title: Quality control inspectors
exposure_rationale: Quality control inspection is primarily a physical, hands-on occupation requiring workers to be present on factory floors, operate measuring instruments, and physically handle products — creating a natural barrier to AI automation. However, AI-powered vision inspection systems, machine learning defect detection, and automated measurement tools are already encroaching on core tasks like identifying defects and reviewing output, meaning AI is meaningfully reshaping parts of the job. The flat employment outlook (0% growth) partly reflects ongoing automation pressure, but the physical presence requirement and need to handle unpredictable real-world manufacturing environments keeps this from being a high-exposure role.

ai_paths.reason: Uses visual inspection and measurement to ensure product integrity.
Applies technical blueprint reading and defect detection skills.
Transfers attention to detail and surface finish evaluation.
Monitors equipment performance and maintains strict safety standards.
Utilizes diagnostic testing and systematic troubleshooting of components.
Requires precise measurements and verification of structural specifications.
Applies quality standards and sensory inspection to products.

education_detail: Quality control inspectors typically need a high school diploma for entry-level jobs. Postsecondary certificate programs are available for instruction on quality control concepts, such as inspection planning and auditing. Students in these programs also gain familiarity with tools and technologies that quality control inspectors use. Some employers require or prefer to hire candidates who have an associate’s or bachelor’sdegreein a field such as quality control management orengineering.
certifications: Although optional, certification may demonstrate a level of competence and professionalism that makes candidates more attractive to employers. It also may increase opportunities for advancement. Requirements for certification generally include a certain number of years of experience in the field and passing an exam. Information on certifications is available fromCareerOneStop.

title: Radiation therapists
exposure_rationale: Radiation therapists perform a mix of technical/clinical tasks and patient-facing work that requires physical presence — positioning patients, operating linear accelerators, and monitoring reactions in real time. AI can assist with treatment planning, tumor localization, and record-keeping, but the hands-on patient care, equipment operation, and interpersonal support for cancer patients remain human-dependent. The slow projected job growth partly reflects efficiency gains from improved radiotherapy techniques, but the physical and clinical nature of the core role limits how much AI can directly displace these workers.
education_detail: Employers usually prefer to hire applicants who have an associate's degree or a bachelor'sdegreein ahealthcare and related field, such as radiation therapy, or inscience technologiesorbiology. However, candidates may qualify for some positions by completing a certificate program. Radiation therapy programs include courses in radiation therapy procedures and the scientific theories behind them. These programs often include experience in a clinical setting and courses such as human anatomy and physiology, physics, and algebra.
certifications: In most states, radiation therapists must be licensed or certified. Requirements vary by state but may include graduating from an accredited radiation therapy program and passing an exam or earning certification from a professional organization. To become certified, an applicant typically must earn an associate’s or higher degree from an approved radiation therapy program, adhere to ethical standards, and pass the certification exam. The exam covers topics such as radiation protection, treatment planning, and patient care and education. Many jobs also require cardiopulmonary resuscitation (CPR) or basic life support (BLS) certification. Information on certifications is available fromCareerOneStop.

title: Radiologic and MRI technologists
exposure_rationale: Radiologic and MRI technologists perform substantial physical work — positioning patients, operating heavy equipment, and providing hands-on patient care — that cannot be automated away. However, AI is increasingly capable of assisting with image analysis and quality assessment, and could reduce the interpretive judgment component of the role. The core physical presence requirement (patient preparation, equipment operation, patient interaction) provides a meaningful barrier to full AI displacement, keeping this in the moderate exposure range.

ai_paths.reason: Expertise in patient positioning, safety protocols, and clinical imaging.
Strong foundation in anatomy, patient care, and emergency response.
Experience maintaining sterile environments and assisting in clinical procedures.
Knowledge of musculoskeletal anatomy and direct patient physical support.
High-stakes technical operation and emergency medical service experience.

education_detail: High school students who are interested in radiologic or MRI technology should take courses that focus on math and science, such as anatomy, biology, chemistry, and physics. An associate’s degree is the education typically required for radiologic and MRI technologists. There also are postsecondary education programs that lead to graduate certificates or bachelor’s degrees inhealthcare and relatedfields andscience technologies. Depending on thefield of degree, education programs may include both academic study and clinical work. Coursework includes anatomy, pathology, patient care, radiation physics and protection, and image evaluation. Some states require candidates for licensure to complete an accredited program.
certifications: Most states require radiologic technologists to be licensed or certified. Few states require licensure for MRI technologists. Requirements vary by state. To become licensed, technologists usually must graduate from an accredited program and either pass a certification exam from the state or obtain certification from a credentialing organization. Technologists may be certified in multiple specialties. For specific licensure requirements for radiologic technologists and MRI technologists, contact the state’s health board. Employers typically require or prefer prospective technologists to be certified even if the state does not require it. Employers also may require or prefer that prospective technologists have certification in cardiopulmonary resuscitation (CPR) or basic life support (BLS). Information on certifications is available fromCareerOneStop.

title: Railroad workers
exposure_rationale: Railroad work is fundamentally physical and operational — driving trains, coupling/uncoupling cars, monitoring tracks, and managing rail yards all require physical presence and real-time situational awareness in dynamic environments. AI and automation can assist with scheduling, dispatching, and monitoring systems, and some roles (like locomotive firers) have already been largely automated away, but the core operational tasks still require human operators, especially given safety regulations and certification requirements. The BLS itself notes that automation efforts are limiting employment growth, suggesting modest indirect exposure, but the physical, safety-critical nature of the work creates a natural ceiling on AI displacement.
education_detail: Rail companies typically require workers to have at least a high school diploma or equivalent. However, employers may prefer to hire workers who have postsecondary education, such as coursework, a certificate, or an associate’s or bachelor’s degree.
certifications: Locomotive engineers and conductors must be certified by theFederal Railroad Administration(FRA). The certifications, conducted by the railroad that employs them, involve a written knowledge test, a skills test, and a supervisor determination that the engineer or conductor understands all physical aspects of the particular route on which he or she will be operating. Engineers who change routes must be recertified for the new route. Even engineers and conductors who do not switch routes must be recertified every few years. At the end of the certification process, the engineer must pass a vision and hearing test. Conductors who operate on national, regional, or commuter railroads are also required to become certified. To receive certification, new conductors must pass a test that has been designed and administered by the railroad and approved by the FRA. In addition, railroad workers must be at least 21 years of age and pass a background test. They must also pass random drug and alcohol screenings over the course of their employment.

title: Real estate brokers and sales agents
exposure_rationale: Real estate involves significant digital knowledge work — market analysis, document preparation, property listings, client communication — where AI can already provide substantial assistance. However, the core value proposition relies heavily on physical property tours, local relationship networks, trust-building with clients during high-stakes emotional decisions, and in-person negotiation, which creates a meaningful barrier to full AI displacement. AI will likely make individual agents more productive (automated listings, AI-driven market analysis, document generation) but the irreducibly human and physical elements of the transaction process moderate the overall exposure.

ai_paths.reason: Applies site knowledge and project coordination to building oversight.
Leverages local area expertise and high-level client service abilities.
Utilizes knowledge of building structures and local housing codes.
Transfers core persuasion, negotiation, and customer service techniques.
Applies business operations and client satisfaction skills to hospitality.
Uses sales presentation skills and deep local area knowledge.

education_detail: In addition to having a high school diploma, real estate brokers and sales agents must complete some real estate courses to be eligible for licensure. Although most brokers and agents must take state-accredited prelicensing courses to become licensed, some states waive this requirement if the candidate has taken college courses in real estate. Some community colleges and 4-year universities offer courses, degree programs, or certificate programs in real estate. These postsecondary credentials typically are not required, but many real estate brokers and sales agents have a bachelor’sdegree. Courses in finance, business administration, economics, and law also may be useful. Prospective brokers who plan to open their own company may find it helpful to take business courses, such as marketing and accounting. In addition to offering prelicensing courses, many real estate associations have courses and professional development programs for both beginners and experienced agents. These courses cover a variety of topics, such as real estate fundamentals, real estate law, and mortgage financing.
certifications: All states require real estate brokers and sales agents to be licensed. Minimum requirements for candidate licensure vary by state but typically include being at least age 18, having a high school diploma or equivalent, completing prelicensing courses, and passing an exam. Some states have additional requirements, such as passing a background check. Licenses typically are not transferable between states. However, some states have reciprocity agreements that streamline the process for brokers and agents licensed in one state to get a license in another state. For a broker’s license, states typically require that candidates have a specified number of years of experience as a licensed sales agent and take additional formal training. In some states, a bachelor’s degree may be substituted for some experience or training requirements. State licenses typically must be renewed every 2 to 4 years. In most states, brokers and agents must complete continuing education courses to renew their license. Prospective brokers and agents should verify requirements with the real estate licensing commission of the state in which they wish to work.

title: Receptionists
exposure_rationale: Receptionists perform a mix of digital tasks (scheduling, data entry, correspondence, phone handling) that AI can increasingly automate through chatbots, virtual assistants, and automated scheduling systems, and the BLS itself notes employment is constrained by automation. However, a significant portion of the role involves physical presence — greeting visitors, controlling access, escorting guests, and providing in-person customer service — which creates a natural barrier to full AI displacement. The job sits at moderate-to-high exposure: the information-processing tasks are highly automatable, but the physical front-desk presence and interpersonal elements keep it from reaching the very high range.

ai_paths.reason: Direct transfer of guest relations and service coordination skills.
Combines administrative scheduling with basic clinical patient interaction.
Utilizes organizational accuracy and customer-facing communication skills.
Applies interpersonal communication to support community client needs.
Transitions office management skills into a clinical patient-care setting.
Leverages public speaking and information dissemination abilities.
Uses verbal communication to navigate and explain complex systems.
Pivots hospitality and safety coordination to mobile environments.

education_detail: Receptionists typically need a high school diploma or equivalent, and employers may prefer to hire candidates who have experience with certain computer software. Proficiency in word processing and spreadsheet applications may be particularly helpful.

title: Recreation workers
exposure_rationale: Recreation workers primarily perform hands-on, in-person activities — leading sports, arts and crafts, camps, and group programs — that require physical presence and real-time human interaction. AI can assist with minor administrative tasks like scheduling, newsletters, or paperwork, but the core job of motivating, instructing, and supervising participants in physical settings is largely insulated from AI automation. The occupation's reliance on interpersonal skills, physical activity facilitation, and adaptability in unpredictable group environments creates a strong natural barrier to AI displacement.
education_detail: Recreation workers typically need at least a high school diploma or equivalent to enter the occupation. Requirements vary based on the type of position. For some jobs, such as organizing after-school programs at a community center, candidates may qualify with a high school diploma and receive training of a month or less after they are hired. For other positions, such as coordinating activities at a residential care facility, employers may prefer to hire candidates who have a bachelor’sdegreeinrecreation and fitnessor a related field.
certifications: Some employers require recreation workers to have certifications such as cardiopulmonary resuscitation (CPR) or first aid. Employers also may require recreation workers to have a valid driver’s license and to pass a background check. Optional certification for recreation workers is offered by professional organizations. Eligibility varies based on education and work experience. Information on certifications is available fromCareerOneStop.

title: Recreational therapists
exposure_rationale: Recreational therapy is fundamentally a hands-on, in-person occupation involving direct physical and emotional engagement with patients through activities like sports, aquatics, arts, and community outings. While AI could assist with documentation, treatment plan drafting, or progress tracking, the core therapeutic work requires physical presence, human empathy, and real-time interpersonal interaction that AI cannot replicate. The high injury rate and physical demands further underscore the embodied nature of this role.
education_detail: Recreational therapists typically need a bachelor’sdegreein ahealthcarefield, such as recreational therapy, or inrecreation and fitness. Recreational therapy programs include courses in physiology, human anatomy, and psychology. Bachelor’s degree programs usually include an internship.
certifications: Employers may require or prefer recreational therapists to be certified. Candidates may qualify for certification in more than one way. For example, one option requires a bachelor’s degree in recreational therapy, completing a supervised internship, and passing an exam. Another option also requires passing an exam but allows candidates with a bachelor’s degree in an unrelated subject to qualify with a combination of education and work experience. In order to maintain certification, therapists must either pass an exam or complete work experience and continuing education requirements after a specified number of years. Therapists also may earn certificates from other organizations to show proficiency in specific therapy techniques, such as aquatic therapy or aromatherapy. Some employers prefer to hire recreational therapists who have basic life support (BLS) or cardiopulmonary resuscitation (CPR) certification. Information on certifications is available fromCareerOneStop. A small number of states require recreational therapists to be licensed or certified. For specific requirements, contact a state’s licensing board.

title: Registered nurses
exposure_rationale: Registered nursing is fundamentally a hands-on, physically present occupation involving direct patient care, administering treatments, operating equipment, and providing emotional support — all of which require human presence and cannot be automated. However, AI can meaningfully assist with documentation, diagnostic support, care plan optimization, and information processing tasks that constitute a meaningful portion of nursing work. The strong projected job growth and irreplaceable physical/interpersonal core of the role limit overall AI exposure, though AI tools will increasingly augment nursing productivity in the knowledge-work dimensions of the job.
education_detail: Nursing education programs usually include courses in anatomy, physiology, microbiology psychology, and social and behavioral sciences. Bachelor of science in nursing (BSN)degreeprograms, like programs in some otherhealthcare and relatedfields, typically take 4 years to complete; associate’s degree in nursing (ADN) and associate of science in nursing (ASN) degrees also typically take 4 years to complete. Diploma programs, usually offered by hospitals or medical centers, typically take 2 to 3 years to complete. There are far fewer diploma programs than there are BSN, ADN, and ASN programs. All programs include supervised clinical experience. In addition to science courses, bachelor's degree programs usually include education in communication, leadership, and critical thinking. A bachelor’s or higher degree is often necessary for administrative positions, research, consulting, and teaching. Generally, licensed graduates of any of the three types of education programs (bachelor’s, associate’s, or diploma) qualify for entry-level positions as a staff nurse. However, employers—particularly those in hospitals—may require a bachelor’s degree. Registered nurses with an ADN, ASN, or diploma may go back to school to earn a bachelor’s degree through an RN-to-BSN program. There are also master’s degree programs in nursing, combined bachelor’s and master’s programs, and accelerated programs for those who wish to enter the field of nursing and already hold a bachelor’s degree in another field. Some employers offer tuition reimbursement. Clinical nurse specialists (CNSs) must earn a master’s degree in nursing and typically already have 1 year or more of work experience as an RN or in a related field. CNSs who conduct research typically need a doctoral degree.
certifications: Registered nurses must have a nursing license issued by the state in which they work. To become licensed, nurses must graduate from an approved nursing program and pass a qualifying exam. Other requirements for licensing, such as passing a criminal background check, vary by state. Each state’s board of nursing provides specific requirements. Information on state licensing board requirements is available fromCareerOneStop. Nurses may become certified through professional associations in specific areas, such as ambulatory care, gerontology, or pediatrics. Although certification is usually voluntary, it demonstrates adherence to a specific level of competency, and some employers require it. In addition, registered nursing positions may require cardiopulmonary resuscitation (CPR), basic life support (BLS), or advanced cardiac life support (ACLS) certification. Information on certifications is available fromCareerOneStop. CNSs must satisfy additional state licensing requirements, such as earning specialty certifications. Contact state boards of nursing for specific requirements.

industries.name: Government, excluding state and local education and hospitals
Educational services; state, local, and private

title: Rehabilitation counselors
exposure_rationale: Rehabilitation counseling is fundamentally an interpersonal, relationship-driven profession requiring empathy, active listening, and in-person client interaction, which creates a natural barrier to AI automation. However, AI can meaningfully assist with administrative tasks like maintaining client records, researching resources, developing rehabilitation plans, and identifying referral services. The core counseling relationship, advocacy work, and nuanced human judgment about client needs and progress remain difficult for AI to replicate, keeping this in the moderate exposure range.

ai_paths.reason: Direct experience supporting individuals with mental and physical disabilities.
Applies developmental psychology and patience to early childhood education.
Crisis intervention and patient assessment skills are highly relevant.
Utilizes behavioral management and supportive caregiving expertise.

education_detail: Many employers require or prefer that rehabilitation counselors have a master’s degree in rehabilitation counseling or a related field. Master’s degree programs teach students to evaluate clients’ needs, formulate and implement job placement strategies, and understand the medical and psychological aspects of disabilities. These programs typically include a period of supervised clinical experience, such as an internship. Some employers hire workers with a bachelor’sdegreeinpsychology,health,social sciences, or another field related to rehabilitation. However, these workers typically cannot offer the full range of services that a rehabilitation counselor with a master’s degree can provide.
certifications: Licensing requirements for rehabilitation counselors differ by state and by type of services provided. Rehabilitation counselors who provide counseling services to clients must attain a counselor license through their state licensing board. Rehabilitation counselors who provide other services, however, may be exempt from state licensing requirements. For example, rehabilitation counselors who provide only vocational rehabilitation services or job placement assistance may not need a license. Licensure typically requires a master’s degree and a specified number of hours of supervised clinical experience. In addition, counselors must pass a state-recognized exam and complete continuing education every year. Contact your state licensing board for more information. Some employers require or prefer that rehabilitation counselors have professional certification, which can help to demonstrate competency in the field. Information on certifications is available fromCareerOneStop.

industries.name: State government, excluding education and hospitals
Local government, excluding education and hospitals

title: Respiratory therapists
exposure_rationale: Respiratory therapists perform highly physical, hands-on clinical work — administering treatments, managing ventilators, performing chest physiotherapy, drawing blood, and responding to emergencies — that requires direct patient contact and real-time judgment in unpredictable medical settings. AI can assist with monitoring data, interpreting diagnostic results, or documentation, but cannot replace the physical interventions and bedside care that constitute the core of this role. The strong projected job growth (12%) further reflects that demand for human presence in this occupation is rising, not declining.

ai_paths.reason: Critical care experience and emergency airway management skills transfer.
Familiarity with sterile environments and patient monitoring is key.
Clinical patient care and specialized medical tool usage transfer.
Understanding of pressurized gas systems and airflow mechanics transfers.
Direct patient rehabilitation and mobility assistance skills are shared.

education_detail: Respiratory therapists typically need at least an associate’s degree in respiratory therapy from an accredited program. Employers may prefer that applicants have a bachelor’sdegree. Some programs require applicants to fulfill prerequisites. High school students interested in applying to respiratory therapy programs should take courses in biology, algebra, chemistry, and physics. In addition to respiratory therapy programs offered by colleges and vocational–technical institutes, an accredited program in the Armed Forces leads to an associate’s degree. Respiratory therapy programs typically include courses in human anatomy and physiology, and therapeutic and diagnostic procedures and tests. These programs also have clinical components that allow students to gain supervised, practical experience in treating patients.
certifications: Respiratory therapists are required to be licensed in all states except Alaska, where national certification is recommended. Licensure requirements vary but usually include passing a state or professional certification exam. For specific requirements, contact a state’s health board. Information on state licensing board requirements is available fromCareerOneStop. There are two main levels of certification: Certified Respiratory Therapist (CRT) and Registered Respiratory Therapist (RRT). Candidates typically sit for the CRT exam. After successful completion, CRTs may take an additional exam to earn RRT certification. Some employers require that candidates earn RRT certification before being hired or within a specified amount of time on the job. Information on certifications is available fromCareerOneStop.

title: Retail sales workers
exposure_rationale: Retail sales workers perform a mix of physical presence tasks (greeting customers, demonstrating products, stocking shelves) and information-processing tasks (answering questions, processing payments, recommending products) that AI can increasingly handle through chatbots, recommendation engines, and self-checkout systems. The ongoing shift to e-commerce already reflects AI-driven displacement, and AI-powered virtual assistants can replicate much of the informational advisory role. However, the physical store environment, hands-on product demonstrations, and interpersonal relationship-building with customers create a meaningful barrier that keeps this from being fully automatable in the near term.
education_detail: Although retail or parts sales positions usually have no formal education requirement, some employers prefer to hire candidates who have a high school diploma or the equivalent.
certifications: Some states require licensure for selected retail sales occupations, such as car salespersons. Aspiring salespersons may be required to pass an exam, complete a background check, or meet other conditions for employment. Requirements vary by state. Contact individual state licensing agencies for more information.

industries.name: Automotive parts, accessories, and tire retailers

title: Roofers
exposure_rationale: Roofing is an almost entirely physical, hands-on occupation requiring workers to be present on job sites, climbing, lifting, cutting, and installing materials in variable outdoor conditions. AI has essentially no ability to automate the core tasks of measuring, removing, and installing roofing materials. Minor peripheral tasks like estimating material quantities or scheduling could see some AI assistance, but these represent a negligible fraction of the work.
education_detail: No formal educational credential is typically required for roofers.
certifications: Most employers require that roofers complete safety certification that meetsOccupational Safety and Health Administration(OSHA) guidelines, either before or after being hired. Roofers may obtain specific certification to qualify for additional work opportunities or greater pay. Information on certifications is available fromCareerOneStop. Some employers require roofers to have a driver’s license to enable commuting to different jobsites.

title: Sales engineers
exposure_rationale: Sales engineers combine technical knowledge with relationship-building and persuasion, meaning AI can significantly assist with proposal generation, technical documentation, market research, and product configuration analysis, but the core value lies in human trust-building and complex negotiation with clients. The role requires substantial in-person interaction, travel, and reading customer dynamics in real time, which limits full AI substitution. AI tools will make individual sales engineers more productive (better demos, faster RFP responses, smarter lead targeting) but the interpersonal and consultative dimensions create a meaningful barrier to wholesale displacement.

ai_paths.reason: Applying technical product knowledge to oversee complex building projects.
Using technical troubleshooting skills to maintain sophisticated medical hardware.
Translating complex engineering concepts for students and professional learners.
Hands-on application of the mechanical principles sold in engineering.
Evaluating technical systems and equipment for regulatory compliance.
Ensuring precision and performance of high-end technical instrumentation.
Supporting aerospace systems with technical expertise and practical knowledge.

education_detail: Sales engineers typically need a bachelor’sdegreeinengineeringor a related field, such asbusiness. However, candidates who do not have a degree sometimes meet qualifications if they have sales experience and technical experience or training. College engineering programs generally include courses in mathematics and the physical sciences. Taking other courses, such as marketing and communications, also may be helpful. College students may have an opportunity to participate in cooperative education programs or internships. Through partnership with local businesses, these programs allow students to gain practical experience while they complete their education.

industries.name: Computer systems design and related services

title: Sales managers
exposure_rationale: Sales managers do substantial knowledge work — analyzing data, forecasting, budgeting, developing strategies, and communicating — all areas where AI tools are rapidly improving and can meaningfully boost productivity. However, the core of the role involves leading and motivating people, building relationships with customers and partners, and exercising judgment in complex interpersonal situations, which remain difficult for AI to replicate. AI will likely reduce the analytical and administrative burden significantly, but the human leadership and relationship dimensions provide a meaningful buffer against full displacement.

ai_paths.reason: Transferable leadership, budgeting, and project oversight skills.
Applying revenue management and team coordination expertise.
Utilizing customer relations and operational efficiency experience.
Leveraging administrative management and staff supervision abilities.
Translating talent evaluation and motivational performance coaching.
Sharing professional industry expertise through structured instruction.
Applying investigative logic and high-stakes negotiation skills.
Managing supply chains, production targets, and business operations.

education_detail: Sales managers typically need a bachelor’s degree, although some positions require a high school diploma. A commonfield of degreeisbusiness, which usually includes courses in management and marketing.

title: School and career counselors and advisors
exposure_rationale: School and career counselors do significant knowledge work (analyzing data, writing reports, researching programs) that AI can assist with, but the core of their job is deeply interpersonal — building trust with students, providing emotional support, and navigating sensitive personal situations that require human empathy and presence. AI can augment tasks like résumé review, career matching, and information gathering, but the therapeutic and relational dimensions of counseling create a meaningful barrier to full automation. The occupation sits at a moderate exposure level where AI becomes a useful tool rather than a replacement.

ai_paths.reason: Uses outreach and navigation skills to connect people with resources.
Combines behavioral knowledge with physical interventions for life skills.

education_detail: Nearly all states and the District of Columbia require school counselors to have a master'sdegree, which is typically in a field such as counseling orpsychology. Degree programs teach counselors the essential skills of the job, such as how to foster development; conduct group and individual counseling; work with support systems, such as parents, school staff, and community organizations; and use data to develop, implement, and evaluate comprehensive counseling programs. These programs often require counselors to complete an internship. Some employers prefer that career counselors have a master’s degree in counseling with a focus on career development. Career counseling programs prepare students to assess clients’ skills and interests and to teach career development techniques. For career or academic advisors, employers may prefer candidates who have a bachelor’s degree and work experience. Master’s degree programs in counseling usually require students to have a period of supervised experience, such as an internship.
certifications: Public school counselors must have a state-issued credential to practice. Depending on the state, this credential may be called a certification, a license, or an endorsement. Obtaining this credential typically requires a master’s degree in school counseling, an internship or practicum completed under the supervision of a licensed professional school counselor, and successful completion of a test. Some employers prefer or require candidates to have classroom teaching experience, or to hold a teaching license, prior to being certified. Most states require a criminal background check as part of the credentialing process. Some states require licensure for career counselors; check with your state for more information. Information on state licensing board requirements is available fromCareerOneStop. Optional certifications for career and academic advisors are available from some professional associations.

industries.name: Junior colleges, colleges, universities, and professional schools; state and local (6112,3)
Junior colleges, colleges, universities, and professional schools; private (6112,3)

title: Secretaries and administrative assistants
exposure_rationale: Administrative assistant work is predominantly digital — document preparation, scheduling, email management, database maintenance, and communication — all tasks where AI tools are already highly capable. The BLS itself notes that AI systems and digital tools are enabling staff to handle tasks previously done by secretaries, contributing to flat/declining employment projections. While some interpersonal elements (greeting visitors, phone calls, coordinating with executives) provide modest barriers, the core work product is fundamentally digital and highly susceptible to AI automation and productivity amplification.

ai_paths.reason: Directly utilizes existing communication and front-desk coordination skills.
Transfers high-pressure communication and detailed record-keeping abilities.
Leverages organizational oversight and vendor coordination experience.
Applies customer service, scheduling, and facility administration skills.
Combines administrative proficiency with basic clinical office support.
Utilizes interpersonal skills and complex task-coordination for guests.
Transfers documentation, scheduling, and client-facing support capabilities.
Pivots organizational and safety-protocol skills into travel service.

education_detail: Some community colleges and technical schools offer courses or programs in a variety of secretarial and administrative assistance fields. For example, courses or programs in office procedures focus on working in a business setting; those in industry-specific terminology and practices prepare students for jobs as medical and legal secretaries. Temporary placement agencies also may provide training in word processing, spreadsheet, and database software. A bachelor's degree typically is not required to become a secretary or administrative assistant. However, some of these workers have a degree in afieldsuch asbusiness,education, orcommunications. Employers may prefer to hire candidates for executive secretary and executive administrative assistant positions who have taken some college courses or have a bachelor’s degree.

title: Securities, commodities, and financial services sales agents
exposure_rationale: This occupation is predominantly knowledge-based and digital — analyzing financial data, communicating with clients, executing trades, and preparing reports — all areas where AI is rapidly advancing. AI has already automated much of routine trading and is increasingly capable of financial analysis, market monitoring, and client advisory functions. However, the high-value relationship-driven aspects (client trust, complex deal negotiation, institutional sales) and regulatory/licensing requirements provide meaningful barriers, keeping this from the highest exposure tier.
education_detail: Securities, commodities, and financial services sales agents typically need a bachelor’s degree to enter the occupation. A commonfield of degreeisbusiness, which usually includes coursework in topics such as finance, accounting, and economics. Summer internships often provide useful experience, and employers may prefer to hire candidates who have worked as interns. Numerous agents eventually get a master’s degree in business administration (MBA), which is often a requirement for high-level positions in the securities industry. Because the MBA exposes students to real-world business practices, it can be a major asset for jobseekers. Employers often reward MBA holders with higher level positions, better compensation, and large signing bonuses.
certifications: Brokers and investment bankers must register as representatives of their firm with theFinancial Industry Regulatory Authority(FINRA). To obtain the license, potential agents must pass a series of exams. Many other licenses are available, each of which gives the holder the right to sell different investment products and services. Traders and some other sales representatives also need licenses, although these vary by firm and specialization. Financial services sales agents may need to be licensed, especially if they sell securities or insurance. Most firms offer training to help their employees pass the licensing exams. Agents who are registered with FINRA must attend continuing education classes to keep their licenses. Courses consist of computer-based training on legal requirements or new financial products or services. Although not always required, certification enhances professional standing and may be recommended by employers. Information on certifications is available fromCareerOneStop.

industries.name: Securities, commodity contracts, and other financial investments and related activities

title: Security guards and gambling surveillance officers
exposure_rationale: Security guards primarily perform physical, on-site duties — patrolling premises, controlling access, responding to emergencies, and deterring crime — that require human presence and real-time judgment in unpredictable environments. AI-integrated surveillance cameras and monitoring systems can automate some observation tasks (particularly for gambling surveillance officers), but the core job still requires humans to physically respond, de-escalate confrontations, and make situational decisions. The BLS itself notes that AI in remote monitoring may limit some employment growth, but the physical and interpersonal nature of the work creates a natural ceiling on AI displacement.
education_detail: Security guards typically need a high school diploma or equivalent, although some jobs may not require formal educational credentials. Gambling surveillance officers also need a high school diploma or equivalent. A bachelor's degree is not required to enter the occupation. However, some security guards and gambling surveillance officers study indegree fieldssuch assecurity and protective serviceorsocial science.
certifications: Most states require that security guards be licensed by the state in which they work. Although licensing requirements vary by state, basic qualifications for candidates are as follows: Be at least 18 years oldPass a background checkComplete training Guards who carry weapons usually must be licensed by the appropriate government authority. Positions for armed guards have more stringent background checks and entry requirements than do those for unarmed guards. Most states require rigorous hiring and screening programs, including background, criminal record, and fingerprint checks, for armed guards. Some states and gaming facilities require a minimum age of 21 to work in a casino. Some jobs may also require a driver's license.

industries.name: Local government, excluding education and hospitals

title: Semiconductor processing technicians
exposure_rationale: Semiconductor processing technicians work primarily in physical cleanroom environments, operating and monitoring specialized manufacturing equipment, handling silicon wafers, and performing hands-on quality control tasks that require physical presence and dexterity. While AI can assist with process monitoring, data analysis, and defect detection, the core job involves physical manipulation of equipment and materials in a highly controlled environment that AI cannot replace. Some peripheral tasks like reviewing process data and documentation have modest AI exposure, but the fundamentally physical and equipment-intensive nature of the work limits overall AI impact.

ai_paths.reason: Precision soldering and material bonding skills are highly relevant.
Monitoring automated systems and pressure controls is a core competency.
Technical manual reading and high-precision assembly skills are shared.
Familiarity with chemical safety and protective gear protocols is essential.
Knowledge of electrical systems and silicon-based technology is beneficial.

education_detail: Semiconductor processing technicians typically need a high school diploma to enter the occupation. High school students interested in becoming semiconductor processing technicians should take classes in mathematics, science, and electronics. However, there are other paths to becoming a technician. For example, some candidates with a high school diploma complete a certificate program that prepares them to operate semiconductor processing equipment. Other candidates have an associate’s degree in a field such as industrial electronics technology or microelectronics. Postsecondary certificate and associate’s degree programs, which may include skills training, are usually offered at community colleges. Some students gain hands-on experience through internships with local semiconductor processing facilities. These partnerships allow aspiring technicians to work in a cleanroom environment and observe processing activities such as wafer handling, quality control, and documentation, as well as to operate equipment. Employers may require candidates to have completed a skills program demonstrating their understanding of semiconductor production equipment.

industries.name: Semiconductor and other electronic component manufacturing

title: Set and exhibit designers
exposure_rationale: Set and exhibit designers do substantial computer-based work including CAD design, drafting, budgeting, and visual planning, all areas where AI tools are rapidly improving and can meaningfully augment productivity. However, the job also requires significant physical presence (overseeing installations, traveling with shows), hands-on collaboration with directors and curators, and creative spatial judgment that remains difficult to fully automate. AI can accelerate the design iteration and visualization phases but cannot replace the on-site coordination, physical problem-solving, and interpersonal collaboration that are central to the role.

ai_paths.reason: Overseeing physical builds, spatial planning, and project timelines.
Managing venue logistics and visual experience for audiences.
Applying aesthetic composition and spatial arrangement to physical media.
Hands-on fabrication of custom structures and detailed set pieces.
Teaching practical design and fabrication skills to students.
Applying fine detail and material knowledge to physical objects.
Utilizing spatial measurement and site mapping for physical layouts.

education_detail: Set and exhibit designers typically have a bachelor’s degree, but theirfield of degreevaries. Common courses of study include theater design and exhibit design,fine and performing arts, interior design, andarchitecture. Employers sometimes prefer to hire candidates who have a master’s degree or allow a master’s degree to substitute for desired experience. Academic programs may train students to research the history, period, and story of a production. They also provide technical instruction in subjects such as reading architectural plans and using computer-aided design (CAD) programs. Other relevant courses include drawing, painting, model building, and hand drafting.

title: Sheet metal workers
exposure_rationale: Sheet metal work is fundamentally a physical, hands-on trade requiring manual dexterity, physical strength, and on-site presence to fabricate, install, and maintain metal products. While some fabrication tasks are already automated via CNC machinery and CADD systems, the core installation and maintenance work requires human workers in unpredictable physical environments. AI may assist with design, planning, or equipment programming at the margins, but cannot replace the physical labor of bending, cutting, welding, climbing, and fitting sheet metal in construction settings.
education_detail: Sheet metal workers typically need a high school diploma or equivalent. Those interested in becoming a sheet metal worker should take high school classes in algebra and geometry. Vocational-education courses such as blueprint reading, mechanical drawing, and welding are also helpful. Technical schools may have programs that teach welding and metalworking. These programs help provide the basic welding and sheet metal fabrication knowledge that sheet metal workers need to do their job. Some manufacturers have partnerships with local technical schools to develop training programs specific to their factories.
certifications: Some states require licenses for sheet metal workers. Check with your state for more information. Although not required, sheet metal workers may earn certifications for several tasks that they perform. For example, some sheet metal workers become certified in welding. Information on certifications is available fromCareerOneStop.

industries.name: Government, excluding state and local education and hospitals

title: Skincare specialists
exposure_rationale: Skincare specialists perform hands-on physical treatments — facials, hair removal, massages, peels — that require direct human touch and in-person client interaction, creating a strong natural barrier to AI automation. AI might assist peripherally with product recommendations, scheduling, or skin analysis tools, but cannot replicate the manual dexterity and physical presence central to the job. The occupation's projected growth further reflects its resilience as a service requiring human contact.
education_detail: To enter the occupation, skincare specialists typically must complete a state-approved cosmetology or esthetician program. Although some high schools may offer vocational training, most people receive their training from a postsecondary vocational school.
certifications: After completing an approved cosmetology or esthetician program, skincare specialists take a written and practical exam to get a state license. Licensing requirements vary by state, so those interested should contact their state board. Many states offer continuing education seminars and programs designed to keep skincare specialists current on new techniques and products. Post-licensing training is also available through manufacturers, associations, and at trade shows. State reciprocity agreements may allow licensed skincare specialists to get a license in another state without needing additional formal training or state board testing. Contact your state licensing agency for details. Information on state licensing board requirements is available fromCareerOneStop.

title: Small engine mechanics
exposure_rationale: Small engine mechanics perform predominantly hands-on, physical work — disassembling engines, replacing parts, using hand and pneumatic tools — that requires physical presence and manual dexterity in ways AI cannot replicate. AI could assist with diagnostics, repair documentation, or parts lookup, but these are peripheral to the core job. The work is fundamentally tactile and location-dependent, placing it firmly in the low-exposure category.
education_detail: Motorboat and outdoor power equipment mechanics typically begin work with a high school diploma and learn on the job, although some of them seek postsecondary education. High school or vocational school courses in small engine repair and automobile mechanics are often beneficial. Motorcycle mechanics typically complete postsecondary education programs in motorcycle repair, and employers prefer to hire these workers because they usually require less on-the-job training.
certifications: Many motorboat and motorcycle manufacturers offer certification specific to their own models, and certification is the recognized industry credential for outdoor power equipment mechanics. Although not required, certification can demonstrate a mechanic’s competence and usually brings higher pay. Information on certifications is available fromCareerOneStop. Motorcycle mechanics usually need a driver’s license with a motorcycle endorsement.

title: Social and community service managers
exposure_rationale: This role combines significant knowledge work (data analysis, grant writing, program evaluation, budget management) with substantial interpersonal and community-facing responsibilities that require human presence and judgment. AI can meaningfully assist with writing proposals, analyzing program effectiveness data, and administrative tasks, but core functions like supervising staff, community outreach, stakeholder relationship management, and navigating complex human service situations resist automation. The mix of office-based knowledge work and direct human interaction places this occupation in the moderate exposure range.
education_detail: Social and community service managers typically need a bachelor'sdegreein social work or a relatedpublic policy and social servicesfield. However, some positions also require a master’s degree.

industries.name: Religious, grantmaking, civic, professional, and similar organizations

title: Social and human service assistants
exposure_rationale: This role is heavily centered on in-person human interaction, relationship-building, and navigating complex social situations with vulnerable populations — tasks that require empathy, trust, and physical presence that AI cannot replicate. While AI could assist with administrative tasks like paperwork, researching available benefits, or coordinating services, the core value of the job lies in interpersonal skills and community-based case management. The occupation's projected growth and its dependence on human judgment in sensitive, unpredictable situations further limit AI's ability to meaningfully displace these workers.
education_detail: A high school diploma is typically required for entry-level jobs. Some employers may require or prefer that workers have an associate’s or bachelor’sdegreein human services or a related field, such associal science,psychology, orpublic policy and social services. Human service degree programs train students to observe and interview patients, carry out treatment plans, and handle people who are undergoing a crisis. Many programs include fieldwork to give students hands-on experience.

industries.name: Local government, excluding education and hospitals
Community food and housing, emergency and other relief services, and vocational rehabilitation services
State government, excluding education and hospitals

title: Social workers
exposure_rationale: Social work is fundamentally built on human relationships, empathy, and in-person interaction with vulnerable populations — qualities that AI cannot replicate. However, AI can meaningfully assist with administrative tasks like case documentation, resource referrals, and record-keeping, and could potentially support assessment and triage functions. The core therapeutic, advocacy, and crisis-response work requires physical presence, emotional attunement, and trust-based relationships that create a strong natural barrier to AI displacement.

ai_paths.reason: Transfers child development knowledge and behavioral management expertise.
Utilizes crisis intervention and mental health support skills.
Applies crisis management and high-pressure decision-making skills.
Transfers community service dedication and emergency response capabilities.

education_detail: Most social workers need either a bachelor’s or a master’s degree in social work. A bachelor’s degree in social work (BSW) is the most common requirement for entry-level nonclinical social worker positions. BSW programs teach students about diverse populations, human behavior, social welfare policy, and ethics in social work. All programs require students to complete supervised fieldwork or an internship. Clinical social workers typically need a master’s degree in social work (MSW). These programs prepare students for work in their chosen specialty by developing clinical assessment and diagnostic skills. Some nonclinical social workers also may complete master’s-level programs. MSW programs generally take 2 years to complete and include a supervised practicum or internship. A bachelor’s degree in social work is not required in order to enter a master’s degree program in social work. Although a bachelor’s degree in almost anyfieldis acceptable, common majors includepublic policy and social services,psychology, orsocial science. Recommended coursework includes sociology, economics, and political science. Some programs allow graduates with a bachelor’s degree in social work to earn their master’s degree in under 2 years. After obtaining an MSW degree, clinical social workers must complete supervised training and experience. The length of clinical training varies by state but may take several years.
certifications: All states require clinical social workers to be licensed. Some states also require nonclinical social workers to have a license or credential. Becoming a licensed clinical social worker requires a master’s degree in social work from an accredited program and supervised clinical experience after graduation. After completing their supervised experience, clinical social workers must pass a clinical exam to be licensed. Because licensing requirements vary by state, those interested should contact their state licensure board. Information on state licensing board requirements is available fromCareerOneStop.

title: Sociologists
exposure_rationale: Sociologists do predominantly knowledge work — designing research, analyzing data, writing reports, and developing policy recommendations — all of which are domains where AI is rapidly advancing and can provide substantial productivity gains. AI tools can already assist significantly with literature reviews, statistical analysis, survey design, and report writing, meaning fewer sociologists may be needed to produce the same output. However, the job retains meaningful human elements such as conducting interviews, building rapport with research subjects, exercising nuanced judgment about social phenomena, and collaborating with policymakers, which provide some buffer against full automation.
education_detail: Sociologists typically need a master’s degree to enter the occupation. Depending on the position, some employers prefer to hire candidates who have a Ph.D. Master's degree programs typically require applicants to have a bachelor'sdegree, but candidates may not need to have majored in sociology or a relatedsocial sciencefield. Courses include research methods and statistics. Many programs also offer opportunities to gain experience through internships or by preparing reports for clients.

industries.name: Educational services; state, local, and private
State government, excluding education and hospitals
Grantmaking and giving services and social advocacy organizations

title: Software developers, quality assurance analysts, and testers
exposure_rationale: Software development is almost entirely digital knowledge work — writing code, designing systems, analyzing requirements, and testing — all domains where AI is advancing rapidly and already providing substantial productivity gains through tools like GitHub Copilot, Claude, and GPT-4. AI can automate significant portions of coding, test generation, debugging, and documentation, meaning fewer developers may be needed to produce the same output, and QA/testing roles face particular disruption as automated AI-driven testing matures. The score is 8 rather than 9-10 because the job still requires substantial human judgment in architecture decisions, stakeholder communication, creative problem-solving, and navigating complex organizational contexts that AI cannot yet fully replicate.

ai_paths.reason: Leverages technical expertise for leadership and project oversight
Uses deep product knowledge to solve client technical problems
Transfers programming and logic expertise to classroom instruction
Applies systems thinking and optimization to physical workflows
Utilizes troubleshooting skills and technical hardware-software integration
Translates complex technical concepts into accessible educational materials
Applies project lifecycle management and quality assurance methodology
Uses technical troubleshooting and digital systems configuration skills

education_detail: Software developers, quality assurance analysts, and testers typically need a bachelor'sdegreeincomputer and information technologyor a related field, such asengineeringormathematics. Computer and information technology degree programs cover a broad range of topics. Students may gain experience in software development by completing an internship, such as at a software company, while in college. For some software developer positions, employers may prefer that applicants have a master’s degree. Although writing code is not their primary responsibility, developers must have a strong background in computer programming. They usually gain this experience in school. Throughout their career, developers must keep up to date on new tools and computer languages.

industries.name: Computer systems design and related services

title: Solar photovoltaic installers
exposure_rationale: Solar PV installation is fundamentally a physical, hands-on trade requiring workers to climb roofs, handle heavy equipment, cut and assemble structural components, and perform electrical connections on-site — tasks AI cannot perform. AI might assist with system design, configuration planning, or scheduling at the periphery, but the core work is irreducibly physical and location-dependent. The occupation's strong projected growth further reflects that demand is driven by physical installation needs that cannot be digitized.
education_detail: PV installers typically need a high school diploma. Some PV installers take courses at local community colleges or technical schools to learn about solar panel installation. Courses range from basic safety and PV knowledge to system design. Although course length varies, most usually last a few days to several months. Some candidates, especially those with construction experience, enter the field by taking online training courses.
certifications: Some states require a license for PV installers. Contact your state’s licensing board for more information. PV installers must travel to jobsites, so employers may require them to have a driver’s license. Although not required for employment, certification demonstrates competency in solar panel installation. Some states require that for projects to qualify for solar-related subsidies, all PV installers working on the projects must have certification. Information on certifications is available fromCareerOneStop.

industries.name: Electrical contractors and other wiring installation contractors
Power and communication line and related structures construction

title: Special education teachers
exposure_rationale: Special education teaching is fundamentally a human-presence job requiring physical interaction, emotional attunement, and individualized relationship-building with students who have disabilities — core aspects that AI cannot replicate. However, AI can meaningfully assist with administrative tasks like drafting IEPs, lesson planning, progress tracking, and adapting curriculum materials, which represent a significant portion of the workload. The job's irreducible need for in-person instruction, behavioral management, and trust-based relationships with vulnerable students creates a strong natural barrier to deeper AI displacement.
education_detail: All states require special education teachers in public schools to have at least a bachelor'sdegree. Some require teachers to earn a degree specifically in special education. Others allow them to major ineducationor a content area, such asmathematicsor science, and pursue a minor in special education. In a program leading to a bachelor’s degree in special education, prospective teachers learn about the different types of disabilities and how to present information so that students will understand. Programs typically include a student-teaching program, in which prospective teachers work with a mentor and get experience instructing students in a classroom setting. To become fully certified, states may require special education teachers to complete a master’s degree in special education after obtaining a job. Private schools typically require teachers to have at least a bachelor’s degree in special education.
certifications: All states require teachers in public schools to be licensed in the specific grade level that they teach. A license frequently is referred to as a certification. Those who teach in private schools typically do not need to be licensed. Requirements for certification or licensure can vary by state but generally involve the following: A bachelor’s degree with a minimum grade point averageCompletion of a student-teaching programPassing a background checkPassing a general teaching certification test, as well as a test that demonstrates knowledge of the subject the candidate will teach All states offer an alternative route to certification or licensure for people who already have a bachelor’s degree. These alternative programs cover teaching methods and child development. Candidates are awarded full certification after they complete the program. Other alternative programs require prospective teachers to take classes in education before they can start to teach. Teachers may be awarded a master’s degree after completing either type of program.

title: Special effects artists and animators
exposure_rationale: This occupation is almost entirely computer-based and digital, with core tasks involving creating graphics, animation, and visual effects using software — all domains where AI is advancing rapidly with tools like generative AI image/video synthesis, AI-assisted animation, and procedural generation. The BLS itself notes that AI may dampen demand by automating routine animation and special effects tasks. While high-end creative judgment, artistic vision, and client collaboration still require human skill, the trajectory of AI in this field is steep and the slow projected job growth (2%) likely understates disruption as AI tools make individual artists dramatically more productive.

ai_paths.reason: Applies 3D spatial modeling and visual storytelling to physical spaces.
Leverages deep understanding of production pipelines and visual narrative.
Transfers technical proficiency with digital media and production hardware.
Utilizes advanced 3D rendering, visualization, and design software skills.
Applies digital sketching, texture mapping, and aesthetic composition skills.
Expertise in animation software enables teaching at the college level.
Uses digital visualization and lighting skills to design environments.

education_detail: Special effects artists and animators typically need a bachelor’sdegreein computer graphics, animation,fine arts, or a related field. Bachelor’s degree programs in computer graphics often include courses in computer science in addition to art. Programs in animation often require classes in drawing, animation, and film. Programs in fine arts may include courses in painting, drawing, and sculpture. Schools also may have specialized degrees in topics such as interactive media or game design. Employers usually prefer to hire candidates who have a good portfolio and strong technical skills, both of which students may develop while earning a degree.

industries.name: Computer systems design and related services
Advertising, public relations, and related services

title: Speech-language pathologists
exposure_rationale: Speech-language pathology is fundamentally a hands-on clinical profession requiring direct patient interaction, physical assessment of swallowing and articulation, and therapeutic relationship-building that AI cannot replicate. However, AI can meaningfully assist with documentation, treatment planning, diagnostic support, and some teletherapy applications. The strong projected job growth (15%) reflects genuine human demand that AI is unlikely to displace, as the core therapeutic work requires physical presence, nuanced listening, and compassionate human engagement.

ai_paths.reason: Expertise in early childhood development and communication milestones.
Managing behavioral needs and supporting developmental growth.
Providing hands-on patient care and personalized treatment plans.
Precise oral cavity work and patient education skills.

education_detail: Speech-language pathologists typically need at least a master's degree in speech-language pathology. These programs usually take 2 years of postbaccalaureate study. Although master's degree programs may not require a particular bachelor'sdegreefor admission, they frequently require applicants to have completed coursework inbiology,social science, or certainhealthcare and relatedfields. Requirements vary by program. Graduate programs often include courses in speech and language development, age-specific speech disorders, alternative and augmentative communication, and swallowing disorders. These programs also include supervised clinical experience. Graduation from an accredited program is required for certification and, often, for state licensure.
certifications: All states require speech-language pathologists to be licensed. Licensure typically requires at least a master’s degree from an accredited program, supervised clinical experience gained both during and after completing the program, and passing an exam. For specific requirements, contact your state’s medical or health licensure board. Information on state licensing board requirements is available fromCareerOneStop. Certification typically satisfies some or all of the requirements for state licensure and may be required by some employers. To become certified, candidates must graduate from an accredited program, pass an exam, and complete a fellowship that lasts several months and is supervised by a certified speech-language pathologist. To maintain the credential, speech-language pathologists must complete a specified number of hours of continuing education. Speech-language pathologists who work in schools may need a teaching certification. For specific requirements, contact your state’s department of education or the school district or private institution in which you are interested. Speech language pathologists may choose to earn specialty certifications in child language, fluency, or swallowing. Candidates who are certified, meet work experience requirements, complete continuing education hours, and pass a specialty certification exam may use the title Board Certified Specialist. Some employers prefer to hire candidates with cardiopulmonary resuscitation (CPR) or basic life support (BLS) certification. Information on certifications is available fromCareerOneStop.

Offices of physical, occupational and speech therapists, and audiologists

title: Stationary engineers and boiler operators
exposure_rationale: This occupation is fundamentally physical and hands-on, requiring workers to operate, inspect, maintain, and repair boilers and mechanical equipment in person. While AI and automation can assist with monitoring gauges and logging data, the core tasks — physical inspection, manual repairs, troubleshooting equipment failures, and operating valves and controls — require physical presence and dexterity that AI cannot replicate. The slow projected employment growth reflects gradual automation of monitoring functions, but the irreducibly physical nature of the work keeps AI exposure very low.
education_detail: Stationary engineers and boiler operators need at least a high school diploma. Students should take courses in math, science, and mechanical and technical subjects. With the growing complexity of the work, vocational school or college courses may benefit workers trying to advance in the occupation.
certifications: Some state and local governments require licensure for stationary engineers and boiler operators. These governments typically have several classes of stationary engineer and boiler operator licenses. Each class specifies the type and size of equipment the engineer is permitted to operate without supervision. Many employers require stationary engineers and boiler operators to demonstrate competency through licenses or company-specific exams before they are allowed to operate the equipment without supervision. A top-level engineer or operator is qualified to run a large facility, supervise others, and operate equipment of all types and capacities. Engineers and operators with licenses below this level are limited in the types or capacities of equipment they may operate without supervision. Applicants for licensure usually must meet experience requirements and pass a written exam. In some cases, employers may require that workers be licensed before starting the job. A stationary engineer or boiler operator who moves from one state or city to another may have to pass an examination for a new license because of regional differences in licensing requirements.

title: Substance abuse, behavioral disorder, and mental health counselors
exposure_rationale: This occupation is fundamentally built on human therapeutic relationships, empathy, and real-time interpersonal interaction, which creates a strong natural barrier to AI automation. AI can assist with documentation, treatment plan drafting, and administrative tasks, but the core counseling work — building trust, active listening, crisis intervention, and guiding behavioral change — requires human presence and connection that clients specifically seek. The strong projected job growth (17%) reflects sustained demand for human counselors, though AI tools may modestly improve productivity in documentation and resource referral tasks.

ai_paths.reason: Uses interpersonal skills to lead therapeutic group activities.
Applies behavioral management and emotional development expertise.
Applies crisis intervention and high-pressure emotional stability.
Utilizes de-escalation techniques and behavioral monitoring skills.

education_detail: Substance abuse, behavioral disorder, and mental health counselors typically need at least a bachelor’sdegree, although the education required or preferred may vary by position, state, or employer. For example, substance abuse and behavioral disorder counselors typically need a bachelor’s degree to enter the occupation, but requirements may vary from a high school diploma to a master’s degree. Mental health counselors typically need a master's degree. Common fields of undergraduate study includepsychology,public policy and social services, andsocial science. Master’s degree programs often include an internship that is clinically supervised.
certifications: Substance abuse and behavioral disorder counselors in private practice must be licensed. Licensing requirements vary by state, but all states require these counselors to have a master’s degree and complete a specified number of hours of supervised clinical experience. In addition, counselors must pass an examination and complete continuing education every year. Licensure requirements for substance abuse and behavioral disorder counselors outside of private practice vary by state. For example, not all states require applicants to have a specific degree, but some require them to pass an exam. All states require mental health counselors to be licensed. Licensure requires successfully completing a national examination and supervised clinical work under the supervision of a licensed counselor. Information on state licensing board requirements is available fromCareerOneStop. Some jobs require counselors to have certification in basic life support (BLS) or cardiopulmonary resuscitation (CPR).

industries.name: Outpatient mental health and substance abuse centers
Residential mental health and substance abuse facilities

title: Surgical assistants and technologists
exposure_rationale: Surgical assistants and technologists perform highly physical, hands-on work in operating rooms — passing instruments, maintaining sterile fields, positioning patients, and directly assisting surgeons during procedures. These tasks require physical presence, manual dexterity, and real-time responsiveness in unpredictable environments that AI cannot replicate. While robotic surgical systems exist, they augment surgeons rather than replace the supporting roles of technologists, and the physical, safety-critical nature of the work creates a strong barrier to AI displacement.
education_detail: Surgical assistants and technologists typically need a certificate or associate’s degree from an accredited program. Many community colleges and vocational schools, as well as some universities and hospitals, offer accredited surgical technology or surgical assisting programs. Surgical assistants may complete a formal education program in surgical assisting. Others may work as surgical technologists and receive additional on-the-job training to become first assistants. Surgical technology education includes courses such as anatomy, microbiology, and physiology. These workers also learn about the care and safety of patients, sterilization techniques, how to set up technical or robotic equipment, and preventing and controlling infections. In addition to classroom study, students gain hands-on experience in supervised clinical settings.
certifications: Certification may be beneficial for finding a job, and some employers may require it. Surgical assistants and technologists may earn certification through credentialing organizations. Certification typically requires completing an accredited formal education program or military training program and passing an exam. In addition, many jobs require that surgical assistants and technologists become certified in CPR or basic life support (BLS), or both. Information on certifications is available fromCareerOneStop. Some states have regulations governing the work of surgical assistants and technologists. For more information, contact your state licensing agency.

title: Survey researchers
exposure_rationale: Survey research is predominantly knowledge work done on a computer — designing surveys, analyzing data with statistical software, interpreting results, and writing reports — all tasks where AI is rapidly capable. The BLS already projects employment decline driven by big data and data mining replacing traditional survey methods, a trend AI will accelerate further. While some human judgment is needed for survey design and client communication, the core analytical and methodological tasks are highly automatable, placing this occupation firmly in the very high exposure range.

ai_paths.reason: Uses similar statistical modeling and population data collection methods.
Applies demographic research to community development and land use.
Transfers advanced subject expertise and research methodology to instruction.
Uses needs-assessment skills to design and evaluate educational programs.
Applies qualitative research and cultural analysis to human behavior.
Leverages interview techniques and data to guide individual outcomes.
Uses data-driven insights to manage and evaluate public programs.

education_detail: Survey researchers typically need a master’s degree or Ph.D. The master’s degree may be in a variety of fields, including marketing or survey research, statistics, or social sciences. A bachelor’sdegreeis sufficient for some entry-level positions. To prepare to enter this occupation, students should take courses in research methods, survey methodology, computer science, mathematics, and statistics. Many also may benefit from taking business courses, such as marketing and consumer behavior, and social science courses, such as psychology, sociology, and economics.
certifications: Although survey researchers are not required by law to be licensed or certified, certification can show a level of professional competence. Information on certifications is available fromCareerOneStop.
